Disposing of an old iPad is not as simple as throwing it away with your regular trash. Electronic waste can be harmful to the environment if not disposed of properly. Additionally, iPads contain sensitive personal information that needs to be wiped before disposal. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to safely and responsibly dispose of your old iPad: ### Step 1: Backup Your Data Before getting rid of your iPad, make sure you back up all important data such as photos, documents, and contacts. This will ensure that you don't lose any valuable information during the disposal process. You can back up your data using iCloud or iTunes. **Key Tips:** * Make sure your device is connected to WiFi when backing up via iCloud. * If you choose to back up through iTunes, connect your iPad to your computer using a USB cable. ### Step 2: Sign Out of All Accounts Sign out of all accounts including Apple ID, iMessage, FaceTime, Game Center, iTunes & App Store, and Find My iPhone. This prevents unauthorized access to your account after discarding the device. **Key Guidelines:** * Go to Settings > [your name]. Scroll down and tap Sign Out. Enter your password and tap Turn Off. * For iMessage and FaceTime, go to Settings > Messages/FaceTime and turn them off individually. * To sign out of Game Center, go to Settings > Games and tap on your Apple ID. Then select 'Sign Out'. * To remove your device from Find My iPhone, open the app, select the device, click 'Remove from Account', then confirm by clicking 'Remove'. ### Step 3: Erase All Content and Settings This step ensures that no personal data remains on the device. It returns the iPad to its original factory settings. **Instructions:** * Go to Settings > General > Reset > Erase All Content and Settings. Confirm by tapping 'Erase Now'. * If prompted, enter your passcode and Apple ID password. The iPad will restart and erase itself. ### Step 4: Physical Destruction (Optional) If you want to guarantee that no one can recover data from your old iPad, physically destroy it. However, this should only be done once you've confirmed there's no way for you to reuse or recycle the device. **Caution:** Physically damaging the iPad makes it unusable, so do this only if you are certain you no longer need the device. **Methods of Destruction:** * Use a hammer or other heavy tool to smash the screen until it's shattered beyond repair. * Drill holes through the screen and battery. * Submerge the pieces in acid or another corrosive substance. ### Step 5: Recycling Options The best way to dispose of an old iPad is to recycle it. Many electronics retailers offer recycling programs, some even offering discounts on new purchases for trading in older devices. Alternatively, look for local e-waste collection events or facilities. **Important Note:** Do NOT throw electronic waste into regular household garbage. These items require special handling due to their hazardous components. By following these steps, you can safely and responsibly dispose of your old iPad while protecting both the environment and your privacy. Looks kinda like a bunch of different animals. Everton's second successful period came after 1961 when Harry Catterick was appointed manager. With Catterick in charge Everton won two league titles and four cups between 1961 and 1970. Title: How to Perform a Soothing Shoulder Massage Introduction: Giving a shoulder massage can be a wonderful way to help someone relax, alleviate tension, and show affection. This article will guide you through the process of giving an effective and enjoyable shoulder massage, complete with detailed steps and helpful tips. Before starting, ensure that both you and the recipient are comfortable - ideally seated or lying down in a quiet space free from distractions. Use lotion or oil if preferred, but make sure your hands are clean and dry before beginning. Step 1: Preparation Wash and dry your hands thoroughly to avoid any potential skin irritation due to dirt or bacteria. If using lotion or oil, pour a small amount onto one hand and rub both palms together to warm and distribute it evenly. This ensures smooth movement during the massage while nourishing the skin. Step 2: Communication Ask the person receiving the massage if they have any areas of particular discomfort or sensitivity. It's essential to respect their boundaries and tailor the pressure according to their preferences. Throughout the session, maintain open communication about comfort levels and adjust as necessary. Step 3: Start at the Neck Base Begin by placing your thumbs at the base of the neck where it meets the shoulders (the trapezius muscle). Apply gentle circular motions with your fingertips moving upwards towards the ears. Repeat this motion several times until feeling some relaxation in the muscles. The aim is to release surface-level knots and promote overall relaxation. Key Tip: Always begin with light pressure, gradually increasing intensity based on feedback from the recipient. Overzealous initial contact may cause discomfort rather than relief. Step 4: Move Outward to Deltoids Next, move outwards along the top edge of the shoulder blade (deltoid muscle) using similar circular movements. Remember to keep your touch relaxed yet firm enough to reach deeper tissues without causing pain. Focusing on these larger back muscles encourages blood flow, reducing stiffness and promoting flexibility. Step 5: Target Upper Trapezius Muscles Return to the center of the upper back, positioning your fingers just above the spine. Press firmly into the meaty section between the neck and shoulder blades - the upper trapezius muscle. Slowly glide them horizontally away from the spine, releasing built-up tension. Be cautious around bone structures like the cervical vertebrae and scapulae. Step 6: Explore Between Shoulder Blades Place your hands palms-down over the recipient's lower scapular area. Using thumb or finger pads, explore gently between the shoulder blades, searching for tight spots. When found, apply sustained pressure directly onto those points, encouraging tissue softening and stress reduction. Step 7: Finish with Effleurage Strokes End the session with effleurage strokes, which involve long, flowing caresses across the entire back region. These soothe the nervous system, helping the body absorb the benefits of the massage. To perform effleurage, simply place both hands flat on either side of the spine, then sweep them slowly outwards toward the arms. After reaching the edges of the shoulders, return to the start position near the spine. Conclusion: By following these steps, you should now possess the knowledge needed to provide a satisfying and therapeutic shoulder massage. Regular practice combined with continued learning will further enhance your skills, making you increasingly adept at providing comfort and healing through touch. The second force is equal in size to the first force. The forces act in opposite directions. For example, if you jump forward off a boat, the boat moves backward. For you to jump forward, the boat had to push you forward. Newton's third law says that for the boat to push you forward, you had to push the boat backward. Often, people say, "For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction." Can you mix this up with your hands? It has been written and was read in the Arabic language for more than 1,400 years. Title: How to Dissolve Salt in Water Introduction: Dissolving salt in water is a simple process that involves mixing the two substances until the salt granules can no longer be seen or felt, resulting in a homogeneous solution. This phenomenon occurs due to the polar nature of water molecules and their ability to surround and break down sodium chloride crystals into individual ions. Understanding this fundamental concept allows us to appreciate the intricacies of ion-dipole interactions and solute-solvent compatibility. By following these steps meticulously, you will successfully dissolve salt in water with ease and confidence. Materials Needed: 1. Table salt (sodium chloride) 2. Distilled water 3. A clean, dry glass or container 4. Spoon or stirring rod 5. Measuring spoons (optional) Step 1: Choose Your Glass or Container Select a suitable glass or container made from materials such as glass, stainless steel, or ceramic. Avoid using reactive metals like aluminum or copper since they may interact chemically with either the salt or water, potentially altering the taste or composition of your final solution. Key Tip: Using distilled water ensures minimal impurities, which might interfere with the dissolution process. However, tap water is generally acceptable if you don't have access to distilled water. Step 2: Determine Amount of Salt Required Decide how much salt you wish to dissolve based on factors such as desired concentration, available volume of water, and intended application. For instance, dissolving one teaspoon (about 6 grams) of table salt in 250 milliliters of water creates an approximate saturation point at room temperature. Guideline: If unsure about quantities, start small; you can always add more salt later. Step 3: Add Salt Gradually Pour the measured amount of salt slowly into the chosen container filled with water. It's crucial to introduce the solid gradually to facilitate even distribution and prevent undesirable clumping. Key Tip: Sprinkling the salt around the surface of the water encourages faster dissolution by increasing contact area between the particles and water molecules. Step 4: Stir Gently but Thoroughly Using a spoon or stirring rod, gently mix the contents to ensure complete interaction between the solvent (water) and solute (salt). Maintain consistent agitation without creating excessive turbulence, which could lead to unwanted splashing or uneven dispersal. Key Guideline: Patience is essential during this stage - rushing through stirring may result in residual undissolved salt at the bottom of your container. Step 5: Observe Saturation Point Continue adding salt and stirring until no more seems to dissolve, reaching what scientists call a state of saturation. At this juncture, any additional salt added would remain suspended as visible crystals rather than blending uniformly with the liquid. Key Tip: Agitating the mixture vigorously after achieving saturation does not increase its capacity to hold further dissolved salts; instead, it causes temporary suspension of excess salt crystals before they settle back down. Conclusion: By diligently adhering to these guidelines, you now possess the knowledge and skills necessary to effectively dissolve salt in water. Whether preparing a culinary delight, conducting scientific experiments, or quenching curiosity about basic chemistry principles, remember that practice makes perfect. Happy experimenting! no, no don't let Paul get it. [Adam found Scotch tap in Ursula's bag] Then won't give the shade that the animals need. And then you can tell him you liked the book. Hippocrates and other doctors at that time treated people based on the humor theory. This theory said that there were four types of fluid in the body (black, yellow bile, blood, and phlegm). Doctors tried to figure out whether these four "humors" (or body fluids) were in balance. They would then use treatments like blood-letting (cutting the patient and letting him bleed so that he would lose blood); laxatives (giving the patient foods or herbs to make him go to the bathroom), and/or changing the patient's diet. The doctors thought that these treatments would work to get the patient's four humors back into the right balance. The humor theory treatment was popular until the 19th century (the 1800s), when cells were discovered. By this time, people had realized that cancer can happen anywhere in the body. CHANGE your habits - SAVE ENERGY Angela J : Hello kids! Are you ready to answer to a very easy question? Students : Yess :) Angela J : Where is the light that is brightening our classroam in the moment coming from? Students : It's coming from the sun. Angela J : Yes, exactly! The Sun gives us light, heat and energy. The plants absorb the light from the Sun and with its help they grow. Then we eat the plants so we grow too. Angela P : But, kids what happens when the Sun goes down? There's no light and there's no warmth... It's dark and it's cold! What do we do then? Students : We switch on the lights and we turn on the warming. Angela P : That's true! What kind of energy do they use? Students : Electricity. Monika D : Yes, that's true! Angela, let tell them that the electiricy we use in mainly is got from the coal and the petroleum. And they are often wasting in big quantities and there's a big possibility when we'll abults there will be no energy. Just imagine kids, what would we do without electricity..?! Angela P : But...there's one more big problem. While getting electicity from the coal and the petroleum, the environment is polluting a lot and the whole planet. That's why we need to be careful when we use the energy. Are you ready to save energy too? Students : Yees!!! Angela J : I have an idea! The biggest saver of energy in our school is Shtedlivko. Would you like to call him to tell us how we can save energy? Students : Yeees!! (Vlado comes in the classroom) : Hello kids! I'm Shtedlivko - the biggest energy saver and protector of the environment. Are you ready to know how do I save energy? Vlado: Well here are some ways for saving energy that I'm using them: - First when the weather is sunny do not switch on the lights. That's the simplest way for saving energy. Switch on the lights when you need them, and the one who last leaves the classroom should turn off the lights! - Second, if you don't use the computers.. turn them off! - If the warming is turned on, close the doors and the windows. Ventilate shortly with wide open window or door. Using this way you'll keep the heat in the classroom. - Big part of the waste that you throw like the paper and the plastic can recycle. And that means that from the old papers and plastic can be remade new products. Then, it's wasted less energy. Therefore, collect the waste on special place, the plastic bottles in special ash bin for plastic, by the way there is one ash bin for plastic in the school yard, and collect the papers in a special place that you'll choose it with your teacher. That's all, kids. Who is ready to join me in saving energy? And what are we going to get from that? These may be somewhat sine-wave in form. Typically, over time, the meanders don't disappear but gradually migrate downstream. However, if some resistant material slows or stops the downstream movement of a meander, a stream may erode through the neck between two legs of a meander to become temporarily straighter, leaving behind an arc-shaped body of water termed an oxbow lake or bayou. A flood may also result in a meander being cut through in this way. " Oh the wonderful sounds Mister Brown can do." Now the baby bird did not walk he ran! Title: How to Get Permanent Juggernog in Call of Duty: Black Ops III Introduction: Permanent Juggernog is one of the most sought-after perks in Call of Duty: Black Ops III's Zombies mode. This perk provides players with double health, making them more resilient against hordes of zombies. Unlike traditional Juggernog machines that require players to purchase refills after every round, Permanent Juggernog remains active throughout the entire game session without any additional cost. To obtain this desirable upgrade, you must complete an Easter Egg quest across multiple maps. This tutorial will guide you through obtaining Permanent Juggernog in detail, providing clear instructions, helpful tips, and crucial information for success. Sorting and Classifying What you need: - insect guide and spider guide (from the library) - magnifying glass - pen or pencil What to do: - With your child, search your home and neighborhood for bugs. Places to look include: in corners, around the front door, in the backyard, in sidewalk cracks, on plants, on lights. - When you find a bug, observe it closely with the magnifying glass. Use the guides to identify it. Then record your findings. Your child may also want to draw the bug. - Collect observations over the course of a week, or maybe even a month. Then create a bar graph to show what kinds of bugs you found. What kinds were most common? Can you think of any reason why you found more of this type than others? - strengthens observation skills - enhances understanding of the ways bugs are classified Recommended Products for Your Child Ages 3-5 oh, yes you can see go right through there now. oh, I bet the cow would like some. Force an Article to Open with the Advanced Editor in wikiHow Editing articles on wikiHow can be done using either the Simple or Advanced Editors. While the Simple Editor is user-friendly and great for beginners, certain formatting options are only available through the Advanced Editor. This tutorial will guide you through forcing an article to open directly in the Advanced Editor when editing, providing more convenience during your editing process. **Step 1: Log into Your Account** Firstly, ensure that you have a wikiHow account by visiting <https://www.wikihow.com/> and clicking 'Sign up' if needed. After signing up or logging in, proceed to Step 2. *Key tip*: If you don't want to create a new account, consider guest editing; however, note that this won't save any preferences like editor choice. **Step 2: Navigate to Preferences Page** Click on your username located at the top right corner after logging in, then select 'Preferences.' This action takes you to a page where various settings related to your wikiHow experience can be customized. **Step 3: Locate the 'Editing' Section** On the left sidebar under 'Personal Settings', click on 'Editing'. It should be around halfway down the list. This section contains all the necessary options for configuring your preferred editing environment. **Step 4: Change Default Edit Mode** Underneath 'Editor,' there is a dropdown menu labeled 'Default edit mode.' Clicking on it reveals two options - 'Simple Editor' and 'Advanced Editor.' Select 'Advanced Editor' from here. *Guideline*: Changing this setting affects every subsequent time you choose to edit a page unless altered again. There's no need to force individual pages to load in the advanced view now. **Step 5: Save Changes** After selecting 'Advanced Editor' as your default option, scroll down to find the blue button reading 'Save & Continue.' Let me try my best to simplify the concept of limits and one-sided limits using everyday language and examples. --- Imagine you have a friend who lives near your school. Every day, you walk towards their house from different directions, but you always stop when you get close enough to see them waving at you through the window. You keep track of how far you are from their house each time you stop. Now let's talk about three interesting questions regarding this situation: 1. As you get closer and closer to your friend's house, do your stopping distances eventually settle down to a specific number? (This is similar to asking whether the limit of the function `f(t)` as `t` approaches `x` exists.) 2. What happens if you always approach your friend's house from the left side of the street? Do your stopping distances still settle down to a specific number? (This is like finding the left-handed limit, or `f(x-)`, where we consider approaching `x` only from values smaller than it.) 3. Similarly, what happens if you always approach your friend's house from the right side of the street? Is there another specific number that your stopping distances tend to? (This is analogous to finding the right-handed limit, or `f(x+)`, considering approaching `x` solely with values larger than it.) The cool thing is that these questions relate closely to the idea of one-sided limits discussed in Walter Rudin's book. In our example, if both the left-handed and right-handed limits exist and equal each other, then just like magic, those numbers will also match the answer to the first question-the overall limiting distance! This means that no matter which direction you come from, you'll end up consistently stopping at almost the same spot every time. So, even without fancy math symbols, remember that understanding how things change while getting closer to something special (like your friend's house) from various angles helps us better comprehend situations involving functions and limits. And hey, maybe next time you play hide-and-seek, you could secretly apply some of this new knowledge! On 15 December 2020, he was hospitalized in Buenos Aires for a urinary tract infection. MOT, IN, and Chi have a long discussion about Chi's paternal grandfather who has melanoma and was taken to the hospital that morning.] Moon and Weight What would happen to your weight if you went on the moon? Why does this happen? Weight is what you get when you stand on a scale. Scales are weight measuring devices that use a spring or some kind of metal bar that stretches or bends when your weight is applied to it. The heavier you are, the greater the amount of stretch or bend. That flexing is transferred to the dial of the scale so you can read your weight. The moon is much smaller than the earth. As a result, the force of gravity on the moon is only about one sixth as strong as gravity on earth. Gravity is what holds us down on the earth's (or moon's) surface. If you were to weigh yourself on a scale here on earth and then could take that same scale to the moon and weigh yourself there, the weight read on the moon would be 1/6 your earth weight. So, If you weighed 100 pounds on earth, you would weigh only about 16 pounds on the You have a certain mass. Mass is how much stuff you are made of, and it is the same whether you are on Earth, on the moon, on Mercury, or anywhere else you can think of. You are held on Earth by gravity. Gravity pulling on your mass is your weight (you will learn in school about gravity as a force, if you have not already). The amount gravity pulls on you anywhere depends on your mass and the mass of the other object (and how far apart the objects are). The moon is smaller than Earth and has a smaller mass, so the moon won't pull on you as much if you are standing on it. The moon's gravity is about 1/6 that of Earth's gravity. If you weigh 60 pounds on Earth, fly to the moon with a scale and stand on it, you will weigh about 10 pounds. If you weigh 120 pounds on Earth, you will weigh about 20 pounds on the moon. If you want to figure out how much YOU would weigh on the moon, take YOUR weight and divide it by 6. Weight is caused by the mass of two objects that are near each other. The bigger the objects, the more the force of gravity. (And gravity is what causes weight.) If you weigh 100 pounds on the earth, you would weigh more on a larger planet... like Jupiter where you would weigh about 236 pounds. On the moon, because the moon is much smaller than the earth (but remember, you are the same size) you would weigh about 17 pounds. Think what a great high jumper you could be! However, in order to survive on the moon you would need a survival suit that could weigh as much as 300 earth pounds... or about 50 pounds more on the moon. Even with that, you and your suit would only weigh about 67 pounds. That is lighter than you are as you sit reading this note. Have you seen movies of men walking on the moon? Do they appear to be lighter? They are. More than that, because the moon has less mass (and therefore less gravitational force) when something falls, it falls more Are you ready for the trip to the moon. I have been for a long time. More than that, it seems there is an interest in returning there. Let's go! P. S. If you want to calculate your weight in other parts of the solar system, you can at this web site - Before we get into answering your question, there are 2 important definitions that need to be brought into the conversation. These include the definitions of weight and mass. All too often these terms are used interchangeably, which drives most scientists up a tree! Mass is the amount of matter (material) that makes up an object. The tricky definition of mass deals with inertia, which is probably a bit beyond where you are at in science class. Technically mass is a measure of how much inertia an object displays - but if that confuses you don't worry about it for now. :) The important thing to remember about mass is that it is constant, and therefore if you are on Earth, the Moon, Mars, or anywhere, your mass is always going to be the same. Weight is defined as the product of a body's mass and the gravitational acceleration in which the mass is located. (Weight = Mass x Gravity) What this means is that your weight depends on both your mass (which doesn't change) and the gravitation force of the place you are located. Therefore, a place with less gravity would cause an object to have less weight. So on the moon, where there is less gravity, you would weight less than you do on Earth where there is more gravity. (Have you ever seen a video of a Moon walk? The astronauts, when they step they look like they are bounce around? That's because there is so much less gravity on the Moon. It looks funny because we are use to seeing people walk on Earth where there is more Let's look at an example, but we'll use the metric measuring system because it is the best measuring system to use in science. Let's say we have a 1kg object (a kilogram (kg) is a measure of mass). The value of gravitational acceleration on Earth = 9.8m/s^2. On the moon, gravitation acceleration is 1.67 m/sec^2. So how much does that object weight on the Earth verses the Moon? 1kg X 9.8m/s^2 = 9.8 Newtons 1kg X 1.67m/s^2 = 1.67 Newtons Although the units used in this calculation may look confusing (in a few years you'll be a wiz at the metric system), you can nonetheless see that on the Moon a 1kg object weighs MUCH LESS than it does on the Earth! Now, isn't that much better than any diet you've ever heard of! Anyone feeling the need to lose weight simply needs to be sent to the moon ;) hehehe . . . I crack myself up. I hope that helps, and good luck! Your weight would be 1/6th of what it is on the Earth. This is because the mass of the Moon is 1/6th that of the Earth. Weight is actually the measure of gravity pulling on you. On Earth, the Earth pulls on you, and you pull on it. The amount of gravity is determined by the size of the two objects pulling on each other. The moon is about 1/6 as big as the Earth. So it pulls on you with about 1/6 the force. So if you could go to the moon, you would weigh about 1/6 of what you weigh on Earth. Weird isn't it?! Now lets say you get to travel to Jupiter. Jupiter is A LOT bigger than the Earth, so you weigh a lot more because Jupiter would be pulling on you with a greater force. One more weird thing to think about. You wouldn't get skinnier by going to the moon, or fatter by going to Jupiter. The amount of stuff that makes you up would still all be there. That's called you mass. Mass never changes. Weight changes depending on where you are in the universe. Your weight actually changes a teeny bit here on Earth if you are on the top of a very high mountain compared with your weight at sea level. I hope this explains a little. There might be some books in the library that would be helpful. Ask your friendly librarian. Good question. The unexplained answer is almost exactly 1/6. But how that happens is more important. Isaac Newton discovered the "universal" law of gravity. At the time, and even today, this law of gravity confuses some people because they confuse MASS and WEIGHT. The MASS of an object is the quantity of matter it possesses. You can ask, "How do we know what that is?" But the answer to that question is too complex to go into here. Just assume that every object contains a quantity of matter, and that quantity is called its mass. Give it the symbol 'M'. The WEIGHT of an object is the force exerted upon it by all other objects in the WHOLE UNIVERSE!! Give that mass the symbol 'm'. Fortunately, only a few and usually only 1 or 2 objects are close enough to have their effect important. In our case only the Earth, Moon, and Sun are important. If we never left the surface of the Earth, the distinction between MASS and WEIGHT could be used interchangeably, but they are different. If you have ever ridden on a roller coaster, and felt like you were being "squeezed" into your seat, or you were "floating" you experienced the difference in your WEIGHT even though your MASS remained the same. Isaac Newton discovered the formula that connects the WEIGHT, the MASS, and the DISTANCE separating two objects. The formula is: W = G * M * m / In his formula: W is the WEIGHT of the object, M is its MASS, m is the MASS of the other body, R is the distance that separates the two and G is a universal constant. G = 6.67259x10^-11 (m^3*kg^-1*sec^-2) but you don't need this to answer your question, as you will see. Standing on the Earth your WEIGHT depends upon your mass, M, the mass of the earth, m, that remains fixed, and R, how far you are from the center of the Earth. The reason people confuse WEIGHT and MASS is because, so long as you remain stationary everything in the formula W= G * M *m / R^2 stays the same so it doesn't make any difference if you use M or W in comparing two Now back to your Moon question. The ratio of your weight on the Moon, Wmoon, compared to your weight on the Earth, Wearth is: Wmoon / Wearth = G * M * m(moon) / (Rmoon)^2 / G * M * m(earth) / (Rearth)^2. This simplifies by cancellation to: Wmoon / Wearth = m(moon) / (Rmoon)^2 / m(earth) / (Rearth)^2. Notice that the ratio of your weight doesn't even depend upon how much or how little your mass is. The quantities you need then, are: m(moon) = 7.35x10^22 kg, m(earth) = 5.9743x10^24 kg, Rmoon = 1.738x10^6 meters, Rearth = 6.378x10^6 meters. So, plugging these numbers in you find that: Wmoon / Wearth = 0.1668 and 1/6 = 0.1667 Hmmm! Is this a coincidence, or is there some underlying There are a couple of other interesting facts. The local force of gravity, that is the apparent weight of a known mass varies from place to place on the Earth (and other astronomical bodies too) depending upon whether you are on a mountain or in a valley and whether you are standing on dense rock or loose sand. There are instruments that can measure this difference. The most sensitive are pairs of satellites orbiting the Earth that compare their position as the pass over the same spot on the Earth at different times. Using these instruments, scientists are making detailed gravitational "maps" of the Earth. If you would like to know how much you would weigh on other planets, find the site: http://kids.msfc.nasa.gov/Puzzles/Weight.asp and plug in your weight. The gravitational attraction at the lunar surface is one-sixth of that experienced on Earth's surface ( a 100-pound person on Earth weighs only 17 pounds on the Moon). This difference allows an astronaut to lift a "heavy" life-support system with relative ease. If it were not necessary to carry such a load, an astronaut could jump six times higher on the Moon than on Earth. I hope that this helps answer you question. Be a heavy be a heavy boy. Title: How to Exchange Old Money Exchanging old money is an important process that ensures the continued acceptance and usability of currency notes. This guide will provide you with a comprehensive set of steps to exchange your old money effectively and efficiently. **Step 1: Identify the Currency You Want to Exchange** Determine which type of currency you wish to exchange. It could be outdated banknotes or coins from various countries. Knowing the specific currency is crucial as different nations have distinct policies regarding the exchange of their obsolete tender. **Step 2: Check if Your Money Is Eligible for Exchange** Not all old currencies can be exchanged due to several factors such as age, condition, denomination, series, etc. Research your central bank's website or contact them directly to understand their policy on exchanging old money. Some banks might only accept certain series or denominations within a particular time frame. **Step 3: Gather All Necessary Information About Your Old Money** Once you confirm eligibility, gather essential details about your old money. These may include serial numbers (if applicable), dates, unique features, conditions, etc. Having this information ready will help speed up the exchange process at financial institutions. **Step 4: Locate Banks or Authorized Dealers That Offer Exchanges** After ensuring your old money qualifies for an exchange, find banks or authorized dealers who perform these services. Typically, commercial banks act as intermediaries between individuals and central banks for such transactions. However, some central banks allow direct submission during specific hours or through mail. **Key Tip:** Always choose reputable establishments to avoid potential scams. **Step 5: Prepare Your Identity Documents** Bring valid identification documents when visiting banks or authorized dealers. Accepted ID types vary by country but usually include passports, driver's licenses, national identity cards, etc. Providing accurate identification assists in tracing the origin of the old money and prevents illegal activities like counterfeiting. **Guideline:** Respect data privacy laws by sharing personal info only when necessary. **Step 6: Present Your Old Money** Submit your old money to the teller or representative. They will inspect its authenticity, often using specialized equipment, before proceeding with the exchange. Be prepared to answer questions about how you obtained the old money. **Key Tip:** Never alter, crease, fold, staple, or deface old money as it may render it invalid for exchange. **Step 7: Complete Any Required Paperwork** Fill out any required paperwork provided by the institution. This typically includes forms detailing the amount and type of old money being exchanged along with your identifying information. Keep copies of completed documents for record-keeping purposes. **Step 8: Receive New Currency** Upon successful verification and completion of paperwork, receive new currency equivalent to the value of your old money. Count the received funds carefully to ensure accuracy. **Step 9: Document the Transaction** Keep records of the transaction, including date, place, personnel involved, and any reference numbers given. Store these alongside other relevant documentation about the old money. By following these steps, you can successfully exchange your old money while adhering to legal procedures and protecting yourself from fraudulent activities. Remember, patience and diligence are vital throughout this process. We've told him the names of them in books and stuff like that I don't know if he would uh you know directly recognize. The nose also contains nerve cells that help the sense of smell. When there is a problem with the nose, the whole body can suffer. Title: How to Apply Command Strips Perfectly Introduction: Command strips are an excellent alternative to nails, screws, or tacks when you want to hang items on your walls without causing damage. They come with adhesive that sticks firmly to surfaces yet can be removed without leaving residue. This tutorial will guide you through applying command strips correctly to ensure they hold strongly and remove cleanly. Materials Needed: 1. Command strip(s) 2. The item you wish to hang 3. A damp cloth or baby wipe 4. Optional - tape measure, pencil Step 1: Choose the Right Command Strip Different command strips have different weight capacities. Make sure you choose one suitable for holding the weight of what you're planning to hang. Check the package for the maximum recommended weight. Key Tip: If you're hanging something particularly heavy, consider using multiple command strips evenly spaced apart for added support. Step 2: Clean the Surface Before applying the command strip, make sure the surface is clean and dry. Any dirt or dust could prevent the adhesive from sticking properly. Use a damp cloth or baby wipe to gently wash the area where you plan to place the strip. Allow it to dry completely before proceeding. Guideline: Wait at least an hour after cleaning to allow all moisture to evaporate. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV3/TP/tomtp3.cha = = = To help each child understand that we are wise when we obey the teachings of Jesus Christ. Prepare two signs, using folded pieces of paper as shown: Prepare to sing or say the words to "The Wise Man and the Foolish Man" (Children's Songbook, p. 281) and "Choose the Right Way" (Children's Songbook, p. 160). The words to "Choose the Right Way" are included at the back of the manual. Write some wise actions and some foolish actions on small pieces of paper. Use the actions below and add others that are appropriate for your class. Place the pieces of paper in a small container such as a box, bowl, or bag.Say your prayers.Obey your parents.Be reverent at Primary.Be kind to others.Share.Tell the truth.Quarrel.Say a bad word.Be selfish.Tell lies.Be unkind. Make the necessary preparations for any enrichment activities you want to use. Suggested Lesson Development Invite a child to give the opening prayer. Follow up with the children if you encouraged them to do something during the week. We Can Make Wise Decisions Display the gloves, hat, and shoes (adapt this activity as needed if you are using other articles of clothing). Put the gloves on top of your head. Is this how we should wear gloves? Why? What should we use gloves for? Point out that it would be foolish to put gloves on your head because then they could not protect your hands. Invite a child to demonstrate the wise way to wear gloves. Put the hat on one of your feet. Is this how we should wear a hat? Why? What should we use hats for? Point out that it would be foolish to put a hat on your foot because then it could not protect your head. Invite another child to demonstrate the wise way to wear a hat. Put the shoes on your hands. Is this how we should wear shoes? Why? What should we use shoes for? Point out that it would be foolish to put shoes on your hands because then they could not protect your feet. Invite another child to demonstrate the wise way to wear shoes. Point out that few people would wear gloves, hats, or shoes on the wrong parts of their bodies. But choices about what to wear can also be wise or foolish. Briefly discuss with the children when it would be wise or foolish to wear different articles of clothing (for example, it would be wise to wear gloves when you are going outside in cold weather, but it would be foolish to wear shoes when you are taking a bath). Explain that we make many choices each day. Foolish choices can harm us and make us unhappy. Wise choices help keep us safe and happy. Jesus Christ Wants Us to Be Wise and Obey His Teachings Explain that Jesus Christ often used stories called parables to teach people. He told one parable about a man who made a foolish decision and a man who made a wise decision. Tell the children that you are going to read this parable from the Bible. Read aloud Matthew 7:24-27. How do you think the man who built his house on the sand felt when the storm knocked his house down? How do you think the man who built his house on the rock felt when the storm did not hurt his house? Explain that Jesus compared us to the men in the parable. If we follow Jesus' teachings, we are like the wise man who built his house on the rock. We will be happy. If we do not follow Jesus' teachings, we are like the foolish man who built his house on the sand. We will be unhappy. Help the class sing or say the words to "The Wise Man and the Foolish Man," using the actions indicated below: We Are Wise When We Choose the Right Story and discussion Explain that everyone makes choices every day. We can make wise choices or we can make foolish ones. Tell in your own words the following story about B. H. (Harry) Roberts, who grew up to become a General Authority. Have the children decide whether Harry's choices in the story are wise or foolish. Harry was born in England a long time ago. His family was very poor, so Harry did not have a chance to go to school. He wanted very much to learn to read and write. He thought that if he could have a chance to learn he would not only read books but write them too. When Harry was ten years old, he came to America and crossed the plains with his sister Polly and other pioneers. Harry had many adventures. One day he heard that his group would be crossing a large river the next day. This sounded exciting, so Harry crept out of camp early the next morning and set out to see the river. This was against the camp rules. Do you think Harry's decision to sneak out of camp was wise or foolish? The river was farther away than Harry thought, and he was tired when he finally got there. He lay down and fell sound asleep among some willows. When Harry woke up, all the wagons had crossed the river. He ran to the riverbank and shouted to get someone's attention. He was told to swim across, so he took off his coat and shoes and jumped into the river. He almost made it across the river, but he became too tired, and the captain of the company had to bring him the rest of the way on his horse. Harry was happy to be safe, but there was no way to get his coat and shoes back. Every night he wished he still had his coat, and every day he wished he still had his shoes. Harry had to walk nine hundred miles barefoot. This made his feet very sore. Prickly cactus grew near the trail, and Harry was so hungry that he often gathered it for food. The sharp spines stuck in his sore feet. Polly pulled the spines out while they both cried. Harry cried because his feet hurt, and Polly cried because she felt sorry for Harry. Why was Harry's choice to sneak out of camp a foolish choice? How did Harry feel about his choice afterward? After Harry arrived in Salt Lake City, he finally had an opportunity to go to school. When he was eleven years old, a teacher took him into her school and taught him the alphabet. The only book Harry had was the Bible, and he read it over and over. He studied hard in school and became a very good student. What choices did Harry make? (He chose to learn to read, and he chose to study the scriptures.) Were these choices wise or foolish? When Harry grew up, he became a wise and important man in the Church. He loved to read and enjoyed reading the scriptures. He also wrote many books about the Church. Many people have read his books and learned more about the Church. (See Church News, 19 July 1980, pp. 8-9; and Truman G. Madsen, Defender of the Faith: The B. H. Roberts Story [Salt Lake City: Bookcraft, 1980], pp. 19-21, 37-40, 56-57.) Display the CTR chart and have the children repeat the words with you: "I will choose the right." Explain to the children that when we choose the right we are making wise decisions. Display the signs labeled "wise" and "foolish" on the floor or table. Have the children say with you the word on each sign. Point out that the "wise" sign has a happy face on it because being wise makes us happy. Point out that the "foolish" sign has a sad face on it because being foolish makes us unhappy. Show the container with the small pieces of paper and invite the children to take turns choosing a paper from the container. As each child chooses a paper, read or have the child read the action written on it. Have the child decide whether the action is wise or foolish and place the paper by the appropriate sign. Encourage the children to do the actions they identified as wise choices. What kind of choices make us happy? In order to be wise and happy, whose teachings should we follow? Why is it foolish to disobey Jesus Christ and do wrong? Testify to the children that we will be happy when we make wise choices. Express your appreciation for Jesus Christ and the things he taught that help us be happy. Encourage the children to make a special effort this week to think about their decisions and try to make wise choices. Sing or say the words to "Choose the Right Way" with the children. Invite a child to give the closing prayer. Ask the child to pray that each class member will be wise and choose the right. Choose from the following activities those that will work best for the children in your class. You can use them in the lesson itself or as a review or summary. For additional guidance, see "Class Time" in "Helps for the Teacher." Show picture 2-7, The Wise Men, and have the children tell you about the picture. Remind the children that the Wise Men came looking for Jesus after he was born (see Matthew 2:1-12). Explain that these men were wise not only because they had great knowledge but also because they sought and worshiped Jesus Christ. They made wise choices to follow Jesus. You may want to have the children role-play the story of the Wise Men seeking Jesus. Ask each child to tell you about a wise choice he or she has made recently (give suggestions if necessary). Write each child's response on a separate piece of paper and have the children draw pictures of themselves on their papers. Remind the children to put smiles on the faces of their drawings because making wise choices helps us be happy. Demonstrate the following object lesson (practice this demonstration at home before trying it in class). Eight or ten children's play blocks to build two simple identical houses, as shown: Two deep pans the same size. A large flat rock to put in one of the pans. A mound of sand to put in the other pan. Water in a watering can or pitcher. Pour the sand into a mound in one pan, and flatten the mound. Put the flat rock in the other pan. Show the blocks and explain that you will use them to build two houses. One house will be built on the sand and the other on the rock. You may wish to ask the children to help build the house on the rock. The house should be similar to the house you build on the sand. After the houses are built, have the children imagine that a storm comes and beats upon the houses. It rains hard and the winds blow. What do you think a storm would do to these two houses? After the children have given their ideas, do not comment. Carefully sprinkle or pour water on the house built on sand until the sand slides away and the house falls down. Put the same amount of water on the house built on the rock, and let the children observe that the house does not fall. Help the children determine that building a house on sand was foolish, while building a house on the rock was wise. Read aloud the first part of Helaman 5:12 (through foundation). Point out that this scripture compares Jesus Christ to a rock. Remind the children that following the teachings of Jesus is like building a house on a rock. It is wise. Stern This lesson plan supplements: "How Distant is the Moon--1", section #8c: on disk Shipprc2.htm, on the web | Goals: The student will| Terms: Lunar eclipse. Also: first contact, last contact Stories and extras: About Aristarchus, an early Greek astronomer. "Stargazers" comes back to this astronomer in section (9a) in connection with his estimate of the size and distance of the Sun, which apparently led him to propose that the Earth revolved around the Sun, not vice versa, about 1800 years before Copernicus proposed it. Start the lesson by asking--has anyone seen an eclipse of the Moon? If so--ask if the Moon was full, or half Moon, or crescent? If not--ask: would such an eclipse be more frequent with full Moon, half Moon or crescent? Of course it must be a full Moon. If Sun-Earth-Moon are lined up, the Sun illuminates the entire side of the Moon facing Earth. Why is it much more likely that you can see a total eclipse of the Moon from your location (in a given year, say), than a total eclipse of the Sun? About half the lunar eclipses are total, because the shadow of the Earth is much wider than the Moon, and the eclipsed Moon is visible from the entire night side of the Earth. Eclipses of the Sun, on the other hand, are usually total only in a small local area, because the Moon is barely large enough to cover the Sun. Unless you are lucky enough to be in that area, you will only see a partial eclipse, or none at all. Ancient astronomers knew how lunar eclipses arose--they knew the Earth was round and cast its shadow on the Moon. The Greek astronomer Aristarchus, around 270 BC, found an ingenious way to use such eclipses to estimate the Earth-Moon distance. Here is how. Then proceed with the lesson. Guiding questions and additional tidbits -- What is an "eclipse of the Moon?"? --If r is the Earth's radius, how wide is the Earth's shadow, approximately, at the distance of the Moon? -- If we accept the above approximation of 2r, and if t is the duration of a lunar eclipse in which the Moon passes right through the center of the shadow--what is the velocity V at which the Moon moves through the shadow? (Actually, not only the Moon moves across the sky, but so does the Sun, because of the Earth's motion around it. Strictly speaking, V is the velocity of the Moon, relative to direction of the Sun.) -- Using the same notation, if R is the distance to the Moon, express the time T of one full revolution of the Moon around the Earth. for V must be equal Cancel factors 2 and multiply both sides by T. Divide both sides by r. [Optional] -- Actually, a correction must be made here: why? However, the Sun is not a point in the sky like a sky, but fills a circle about 0.5 wide. When a point on the Moon would observe the Earth covering the middle of the Sun, it still receives sunlight from half the Sun. To darken the Sun, all of the Sun must be covered and this only happens when the center of the Sun is already 0.25 behind the edge of the Earth. Similarly, the shadow ends when the center of the Sun reaches a point 0.25 short of the edge of the Earth, which is when the first bit of the Sun pokes out past the edge. -- Who first made this calculation? I already got you a cup of milk. Title: How to Cope With a Broken Toe A broken toe can be painful and inconvenient, but proper care can help ensure a speedy recovery. This article will guide you through the steps to cope with a broken toe at home. However, if your pain is severe or you suspect that your toe might be dislocated or fractured in multiple places, seek medical attention immediately. ### Step 1: Confirm the Break Key Tip: If you strongly suspect a break, consult a healthcare professional before attempting any self-care methods. Only a doctor can accurately diagnose and treat a broken toe. To confirm whether your toe is broken, look out for these signs: * Severe pain * Swelling * Bruising * Difficulty walking * A toe pointing in an unusual direction (dislocation) If you notice any of these symptoms, contact your healthcare provider for further evaluation. They may take X-rays to determine the severity and location of the fracture. ### Step 2: Rest and Elevate Your Foot Desired Outcome: Reducing stress on the injured toe can alleviate pain and swelling while promoting healing. What You'll Need: * Comfortable seating area * Pillows or blankets Instructions: 1. Find a comfortable place to sit down. 2. Prop up your foot using pillows or blankets so that it is higher than your heart level. 3. Limit weight bearing activities as much as possible for the first few days following injury. 4. Use crutches or a walking boot if recommended by your healthcare provider. ### Step 3: Apply Ice Pack Desired Outcome: Applying ice reduces inflammation and numbs the affected area, thus minimizing pain and swelling. What You'll Need: * Ice pack or frozen peas wrapped in a cloth * Timer Instructions: 1. Place the ice pack on the affected area for no more than 20 minutes at a time. 2. Wait for at least 2 hours between applications. 3. Continue this process several times daily during the first two days post-injury. Guidelines: Never apply ice directly to skin; always wrap it in a cloth to prevent frostbite. Discontinue use if skin becomes red or irritated. ### Step 4: Take Pain Medication Desired Outcome: Over-the-counter medications can help manage pain effectively. What You'll Need: * Ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin), acetaminophen (Tylenol), or naproxen sodium (Aleve). Consult your physician about appropriate dosages based on age and health status. Instructions: Follow package directions carefully when administering medication. Remember, taking more than the recommended dose won't hasten bone healing - it could lead to serious side effects instead. ### Step 5: Buddy Taping Desired Outcome: Properly taping the broken toe to its neighbor can provide stability and reduce movement, thereby easing discomfort. What You'll Need: * Medical adhesive tape Instructions: 1. Clean both toes thoroughly with soap and water. Dry completely. What am I going to make with uh eggs? [MOT retrieves a crayon that has fallen to the floor] I can do I can do it. Freezing the chat on Twitch is a popular feature that allows streamers to temporarily pause incoming messages from viewers. This can be useful during important moments in the broadcast or when sensitive topics are being discussed. By freezing the chat, streamers can maintain control over the conversation and ensure that all viewers have an opportunity to read previous comments before new ones appear. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to freeze the chat on Twitch: **Step 1:** Log into your Twitch account To begin, navigate to the Twitch website (twitch.tv) and sign in using your credentials. Make sure you are logged in as either the broadcaster or a moderator with appropriate permissions to manage the channel settings. **Step 2:** Navigate to Channel Settings Once signed in, click on your profile picture located at the top right corner of the screen, then select "Channel" from the dropdown menu. From there, choose "Settings" which should be listed under the video player window. **Step 3:** Accessing Moderation Tools In the left-hand sidebar within the Settings page, locate and click on "Moderation." This will reveal various options related to managing user interactions within your channel. **Step 4:** Enable Slow Mode Under the "Chat Options" section, find the option labeled "Slow Mode." Toggle this switch to the 'on' position. Once enabled, Slows Mode limits users to sending one message every few seconds. The default setting is usually around three seconds between messages, but you can adjust this time frame based on your preferences by clicking on the gear icon next to "Slow Mode" and inputting your preferred delay period. *Key Tip:* Keep in mind that while slow mode doesn't technically "freeze" the chat like some other platforms do, it serves a similar purpose by controlling the pace of conversations, giving everyone ample time to catch up. Additionally, if someone tries to spam or flood the chat, these excessive messages will only count towards their allowance rather than overwhelming the chat feed. **Step 5:** Customize User Permissions (Optional) If necessary, customize individual user roles and permissions. For example, you might want certain trusted individuals such as regular mods to continue posting normally without any restrictions even when Slow Mode is activated. To do so, head back to the main Setting page, click on "Roles," followed by "Editor" or "VIP" depending on who you wish to modify. Adjust the corresponding permission levels accordingly. **Step 6:** Test Your Setup Before going live, test out your setup to make sure everything works correctly. Send several test messages from another account or ask a friend to help out. Observe whether the messages appear according to the set interval and if the overall flow of communication aligns with what you had envisioned. Remember, freezing the chat on Twitch isn't about stifling viewer engagement but instead promoting meaningful interaction. Use this tool wisely to enhance your streams and create a more enjoyable viewing experience for all participants! No, you height take it out again cause you height [height] do it this way. Imagine you have a bunch of numbers that follow a special pattern called a "lognormal distribution." This means that when we draw a graph of these numbers, it looks like a bell curve that has been stretched out or squished down. Now suppose you want to move all of these numbers up or down a little bit to get a new set of numbers with a certain average value (mean), but still keep the same amount of spread or variation (standard deviation). You might think that you could just add or subtract the same number from each of your original numbers to get the desired result. However, this method won't work because of the way lognormal distributions behave! When we shift a set of lognormally distributed numbers, some of them may end up being negative. But wait - how can a number be less than zero if it's part of a lognormal distribution?! Well, remember that lognormal distributions are based on another type of distribution called a normal distribution, where all the numbers fall along a symmetrical bell curve around zero. When we take the exponential of those normal numbers to create our lognormal distribution, any negative numbers become positive. Therefore, there cannot be any negative numbers in a proper lognormal distribution. So instead, here's what you need to do: 1. First, find the mean and standard deviation of your original lognormal data. Remember, these two values tell us about the shape of the distribution. 2. Next, decide what your new mean should be while keeping the same standard deviation. 3. Unfortunately, you can't simply adjust the mean without affecting the standard deviation in lognormal distributions. That's because both the mean and standard deviation depend on the mean and variance of the underlying normal distribution. But don't worry! There are other ways to manipulate data distributions that will work better for lognormal data. Just make sure not to break the rules of having only positive numbers in your lognormal distribution, and always double-check your results to ensure accuracy. Good luck! Solution: Relative frequency is f/n. f = 25, and n = 35+10+25+45+20 = 135. Dividing 25/135 gives 0.185185... 0.19 or 19% Common mistake: Some students divide 135/25 instead of 25/135. Relative frequency is always a decimal less than 1 (or a percent less than 100%). Common mistake: Some students give the answer as 135/5 = 27. Answer: pie chart and bar chart Common mistake: "Histogram" is not an acceptable answer. A histogram is used for displaying numeric data. Title: How to Be Popular Over Summer Break Introduction: Summer break is an excellent opportunity to increase your social standing and become more popular among your peers. By following this comprehensive guide, you will learn various strategies and techniques that can help you build stronger relationships, gain new friends, and enhance your overall popularity. Remember, true popularity comes from being genuine, kind, and confident. Step 1: Improve Your Communication Skills * Explanation: Effective communication is essential for building strong connections with others. Good listeners are often seen as more likable and approachable. * Tips: + Practice active listening by focusing on what the other person is saying without interrupting. + Show empathy towards the speaker's feelings and thoughts. + Use open-ended questions to encourage conversation and show interest in the topic at hand. Step 2: Expand Your Social Circle * Explanation: Meeting new people during summer break allows you to form new friendships and strengthen existing ones. This diversity can lead to increased popularity. * Guidelines: + Attend local events such as festivals, concerts, or community gatherings where you might meet like-minded individuals. Title: How to Email Multiple Notes in Evernote Evernote is a powerful note-taking app that allows you to store, organize, and share various types of content, including text, documents, images, and audio files. One useful feature of Evernote is its ability to receive emails, which can be converted into notes and added to your notebooks. This tutorial will guide you through the process of emailing multiple notes in Evernote, providing detailed explanations, key tips, and best practices along the way. ### Section 1: Preparation #### Step 1: Create Your Source Notes Before you start sending emails with your notes, ensure you have all the necessary information organized within Evernote. You may create individual notes or group related items together using stacks or tags. Keep in mind that when combining notes via email, they'll become one single note containing all the combined contents. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Use clear and descriptive titles for your source notes so they're easy to identify later. * Organize similar notes by stack or tag for easier access during the emailing process. #### Step 2: Obtain Note Identifiers (NoteIDs) Each note in Evernote has a unique identifier called the NoteID. To combine multiple notes via email, you need their corresponding NoteIDs. Here's how to find them: 1. Open the desktop version of Evernote. Unfortunately, NoteIDs are not accessible from mobile devices or web browsers at this time. 2. Navigate to the note whose ID you want to locate. 3. Click on the 'More Options' button located next to the note title; then select 'Copy Note Link'. The link contains both the NotebookGUID and NoteID separated by an underscore (_). For example: `https://www.evernote.com/shard/s123/sr/456/_789abcdefghij` Key Tips & Guidelines: * Make sure you copy only the NoteID after the last underscore (_), as shown in the example above. * Save each NoteID separately for future reference while composing the email. ### Section 2: Combining Notes via Email Now that you have prepared your source notes and obtained their respective NoteIDs, let's move forward with the actual emailing process. #### Step 3: Set Up Your Recipient Address To send an email containing multiple notes, address it to "<your_email>@m.evernote.com". Replace "<your\_email>" with the email address associated with your Evernote account. If you haven't linked an email yet, do so first before proceeding further. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Double-check the recipient address for accuracy. Any errors might result in failed delivery. #### Step 4: Add Subject Line and Body Text The subject line should contain specific commands telling Evernote what actions to perform upon receiving the email. Here's our recommended format: ```bash Usually we're post go with him [their father]. " Sittin' on chairs one two three. " Yes he's going an' change his clothes. Mom and Dad brought shovels from home and started digging around the edges of the crack. After a lot of work, the crack was filled and the path was fixed. The little girl was so happy and excited! Because they were painted that way or made that color. Title: How to Change Keyboard Layout in Ubuntu Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through changing your keyboard layout in Ubuntu. This can be particularly useful if you are using a non-English operating system or need special characters for programming or other tasks. The steps below are applicable for all recent versions of Ubuntu (18.04 LTS, 20.04 LTS, etc.) and their derivatives such as Linux Mint, elementary OS, etc. Step 1: Accessing System Settings Click on the 'Show Applications' button at the bottom left corner of your screen or press the Windows key to open the application overview. Look for the settings icon which resembles a gear or cogwheel and click on it to launch the 'Settings' app. Step 2: Navigating to the Keyboard Preferences In the settings window, look for the 'Region & Language' option under the peripherals category. Click on this option to proceed. Once inside, navigate to the 'Input Sources' tab within the Region & Language settings window. Here, you will find options related to input sources including keyboard layouts. Key Tips & Guidelines: If you cannot locate the 'Region & Language' setting directly from the main settings page, consider searching for it by clicking on the search bar at the top of the settings window and typing 'Region & Language'. Step 3: Adding a New Input Source To add a new input source (keyboard layout), click on the plus sign (+) located next to the 'Add an Input Source' label. A list containing various languages and corresponding keyboard layouts will appear. Locate the language and layout that suits your needs and expand its submenu if necessary. Check the box adjacent to the preferred layout variant, then click 'Add'. Your newly added layout should now appear in the list of active input sources. Step 4: Changing Between Input Sources Now that multiple input sources have been configured, switching between them is straightforward. There are two primary methods for toggling between input sources: Method A - Status Tray Indicator A small flag representing the current input source appears in your status tray, typically found near the right side of your top panel. Simply click on this flag to reveal a menu displaying all available input sources. Select the desired layout from the dropdown list to switch. Method B - Shortcut Keys Alternatively, you may utilize predefined shortcut keys to cycle through your activated input sources quickly. By default, these shortcuts are Super+Space; however, they might vary depending upon your desktop environment. To verify or modify these hotkeys, access GNOME Tweaks tool via terminal with command `sudo apt install gnome-tweak-tool` and explore the 'Keyboard & Mouse' section. Key Tips & Guidelines: When adding a new input source, pay close attention to subtle differences between similar layouts. For instance, US International includes additional functionality for entering accented characters common in Western European languages. Conclusion: With these simple steps, users can effectively change their keyboard layout in Ubuntu according to their specific requirements. Whether working across multiple languages or utilizing unique character sets, mastering this process enables greater flexibility and productivity when navigating through any Linux distribution based on Ubuntu. Lily was a patient girl. She liked to wait for her mom to come home from work. She would sit on the couch and look at her books. She would wonder what her mom was doing. Was she talking to people? Was she making things? Was she having fun? One day, Lily heard a loud noise. It was the alarm. It was beeping and flashing. Lily was scared. She did not know what to do. She ran to the door and looked outside. She saw smoke and fire. She saw people running and shouting. She saw a big red truck with a siren. Lily wanted her mom. She cried and called her name. She hoped her mom was safe. She hoped her mom would come soon. "Mommy, mommy, where are you?" she sobbed. Then, she heard a voice. It was her mom. She was on the phone. She sounded worried but calm. "Lily, honey, are you okay? I'm sorry, I can't come home right now. There's a fire at my office. But don't worry, I'm fine. The firefighters are here. They are helping us. I love you so much. Can you do something for me?" Lily sniffed and nodded. She was happy to hear her mom. "What can I do, mommy?" "Can you go to Mrs. Lee's house? She's our neighbor. She's very nice. She will take care of you until I come back. Can you do that, honey?" Lily nodded again. She liked Mrs. Lee. She had a cat and a garden. She always gave Lily cookies and juice. "Okay, mommy. I will go to Mrs. Lee's house. I love you too. Please be careful." "Thank you, Lily. You're such a brave girl. I'm so proud of you. I'll see you soon. Bye, honey." Lily hung up the phone. She grabbed her backpack and her teddy bear. She ran to Mrs. Lee's house. She knocked on the door. Mrs. Lee opened it and smiled. "Hello, Lily. Come in, come in. I heard about the fire. I'm so sorry. Your mom called me and told me to look after you. Don't worry, she's okay. She'll be here soon. Do you want some cookies and juice?" Lily nodded and smiled. She felt a little better. She went inside and sat on the couch. She hugged her teddy bear and looked at Mrs. Lee's cat. She wondered when her mom would come home. She hoped the fire would stop. She was patient. She waited. Title: How to Change the Facebook Site's Viewing Language Facebook is available in multiple languages, making it accessible to users from different parts of the world. If you want to change the language that Facebook displays, this tutorial will guide you through the process with clear, detailed steps. ### Step 1: Log into your Facebook account First, open your web browser and navigate to <https://www.facebook.com/>. Enter your email address or phone number along with your password to log in to your account. This step ensures you have access to your account settings, which we need to modify later in the process. ### Step 2: Access Account Settings After logging in, click on the arrow pointing down located at the top-right corner of the screen next to the question mark icon. A dropdown menu will appear; select 'Settings & Privacy.' Then, click on 'Settings' within the submenu to proceed. The 'Settings' page contains all the configurations related to your Facebook account, including the site's viewing language. ### Step 3: Select the Language and Region section From the left sidebar on the 'Settings' page, locate the 'Language and Region' option under the 'Preferences' heading. Click on it to expand the section. Here, you can configure various options like preferred language, date format, time zone, etc., but our focus is changing the display language. ### Step 4: Choose your preferred language Under the 'Language' header inside the expanded 'Language and Region' section, find the current language displayed beside 'What language do you want to use Facebook in?' You may notice a button (either a globe icon or a dropdown arrow) indicating there are more language options. Click on it to reveal the full list of supported languages. Scroll through the list or type the name of your preferred language in the search bar provided above the list. Once you've found your chosen language, simply click on it to set it as your new default view. Confirm any prompts if necessary. ### Key Tips and Guidelines * Changing the language setting applies only to your individual experience-it does not affect other accounts or posts you see on your feed unless they were written in the same language. * Some features might take some time to fully translate after changing the language, so be patient during the transition. * To ensure proper functionality across applications integrated with Facebook (like Instagram), consider using the same language preference on both platforms. * If an application doesn't support your selected language, contact its developer for assistance or choose another alternative. * Remember that certain slang terms or idioms unique to one language don't always translate well into others. Use discretion when interacting with content originally posted in a foreign language. I asked you some type of variation on this but... Then Tarzan chanced to look up and across the clearing. But Tarzan sped by her, and as he went, her eyes followed him and she saw the cause of his sudden descent and his rapid charge across the clearing. But when the Princess Clotilda heard how, even in the midst of his burning and plundering, the young Frankish chief spared some of the fairest Christian churches, he became still more her hero; and again the desire to convert him from paganism and to revenge her father's murder took shape in her mind. Lily and Max were twins who liked to play together. They had many toys, but their favorite was a big vase that their mom gave them. The vase was blue and shiny, and it had flowers on it. Lily and Max liked to pretend that the vase was a castle, a spaceship, or a hat. One day, they had a fight over the vase. Lily wanted to use it as a castle, and Max wanted to use it as a spaceship. They both pulled the vase, and it broke into two pieces. They were very sad and angry, and they blamed each other. "Give me the vase, it's mine!" Lily shouted. "No, it's mine, you broke it!" Max yelled. They stopped playing and went to their rooms. They did not talk to each other for a long time. Their mom saw what happened and felt sorry for them. She knew they loved the vase, and she wanted them to be friends again. She had an idea. She glued the vase back together, and put a sticker on it that said "Lily and Max". She brought the vase to their rooms and knocked on their doors. "Look what I have for you," she said. "It's your vase, and it's fixed. But there is something new on it. Can you see it?" Lily and Max opened their doors and saw the vase. They were surprised and happy. They saw the sticker with their names, and they smiled. "It's our vase, and it's beautiful," Lily said. "Yes, it's our vase, and it's special," Max said. They hugged their mom and thanked her. Then they hugged each other and said they were sorry. They decided to share the vase and play together again. Creating a folder on Google Drive using an Android device is a straightforward process that can help you stay organized by keeping your files grouped together in one place. This tutorial will guide you through the steps involved in creating a new folder on Google Drive via your Android device. **Step 1: Launch the Google Drive App** To begin with, locate the Google Drive app on your Android device's home screen or app drawer. Tap on its icon to open the app. If you don't have the Google Drive app installed yet, download and install it from the Google Play Store first. Google Drive allows you to store and access files in the cloud, sync them across multiple devices, and share them with others. By organizing these files into folders, you can make it easier to find what you need later. **Step 2: Sign in to Your Account (if necessary)** If you haven't already signed in to your Google account within the Google Drive app, you'll be prompted to do so now. Enter your email address and password when asked. After signing in successfully, you'll see all your existing files and folders stored in your Google Drive storage. Signing in ensures that any changes made within the app are synced to your online Google Drive account, enabling seamless cross-platform functionality between different devices where you might also use Google Drive. **Step 3: Navigate to the Location Where You Want to Create the New Folder** Decide whether you want to create the new folder at the root level (topmost) directory of your Google Drive or inside another existing folder. To navigate, tap the hamburger menu (three horizontal lines) located at the top left corner of the screen. Then select either "My Drive" if you wish to add the folder directly under your main drive or choose an appropriate subfolder name from the list if you prefer placing it there instead. Navigating to the correct location beforehand prevents confusion about where the new folder resides once created, making it simpler to manage your files subsequently. **Step 4: Add a New Folder** Once you've reached the intended destination, look towards the bottom right edge of the display and spot the blue circular button with a '+' symbol in the center. Tapping this button opens up a contextual popup menu containing several options; among them, select "Folder." Selecting "Folder" initiates the creation process. Now you should provide a suitable name for your newly formed container. **Step 5: Name Your Folder** A dialog box will appear asking you to input a title for your folder. Use something descriptive and recognizable, as clear naming conventions facilitate quick identification amidst other items. After entering the desired label, hit the checkmark button located at the upper right corner of the keyboard or press "OK" depending on your device interface. The action finalizes the folder creation procedure. Congratulations! You have just learned how to create a folder on Google Drive using your Android device. With well-organized folders housing relevant documents, collaborative projects become more efficient and finding individual files becomes much less time-consuming. Muslims are in many countries. Title: How to Download YouTube Videos on a Mac Downloading YouTube videos on a Mac can be an excellent way to enjoy your favorite content offline or create video compilations. This guide will walk you through the necessary steps using third-party tools while providing essential tips and guidelines along the way. Please note that downloading copyrighted material without permission may violate terms of service and applicable laws; always ensure you have the rights to download and share the content. ### Method 1: Using macOS Terminal (Command Line) with `youtube-dl` **Step 1:** Install Homebrew package manager Homebrew simplifies installing various packages on macOS by offering a simple command line interface. To get started, open up the **Terminal** application found within Applications > Utilities. Then paste this command into the terminal window and press enter: ```bash /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)" ``` This command downloads and executes the installation script from the official Homebrew GitHub repository, which configures Homebrew on your system. For more information about Homebrew, visit their website at <https://brew.sh/>. **Key tip**: If you encounter any errors during this process, carefully read them as they might indicate specific issues related to your user permissions or network connectivity that need addressing before proceeding further. **Step 2:** Install youtube-dl via Homebrew With Homebrew installed successfully, we'll now leverage it to set up `youtube-dl`, a powerful command-line tool used for downloading online media, including YouTube videos. Enter this command in the same terminal window: ``` brew install youtube-dl ``` Wait until the installation completes-this could take several minutes depending on your internet connection speed. Once done, type: ```css youtube-dl --version that's for mommy to touch not Mat. D sseldorf is one of the most important economical centres in Germany. It has a regional stock exchange.
