A black hole is a region of space from which nothing, not even light, can escape. According to the general theory of relativity, it starts existing when spacetime gets curved by a huge mass. There is a sphere around the black hole. If something goes inside the sphere, it can not leave. This sphere is called the event horizon. A black hole is black because it absorbs all the light that hits it. It reflects nothing, just like a perfect black body in thermodynamics. Under quantum mechanics, black holes have a temperature and emit Hawking radiation, which makes them slowly get smaller. No Catherine, I need that I was using that for XXX. If an alloy has only two types of atoms, like copper-nickel alloy, then such an alloy is called "binary alloy". If an alloy has three types of atoms, like iron, nickel and chromium, then it is called a "ternary alloy". An alloy with four types of atoms is called a "quaternary alloy" and an alloy with five types of atoms is called a "quinary alloy". Another important property of a photon is its polarity. If you saw a giant photon coming straight at you, it could appear as a swath whipping vertically, horizontally, or somewhere in between. Polarized sunglasses stop photons swinging up and down from passing. This is how they reduce glare as light bouncing off of surfaces tend to fly that way. Liquid crystal displays also use polarity to control which light passes through. Some animals can see light polarization. " Good night cow jumping over the moon." Help Children Build Strong Language Skills Tips to work with your child to build a strong vocabulary and other language skills. Listen as much as you talk Talk about things your child is already interested in. Make a conversation longer by adding new information or asking a follow-up question. Make a point of introducing your children to other adults or children, telling one another their names. Use meal time to ask about the day's events. Use drive time. (Turn off the radio.) Reread familiar stories, and talk about the book. Follow the child's lead, and encourage a train of thought he or she already has. Comment on what your child is doing. "Oh, you made a big house!" Once upon a time, there was a wild smelly bear who liked to explore. He would travel for many days, sometimes even weeks, before he returned to his cozy cave in the forest. Every time he returned home, he was always excited to find something new. He often found strange places and things that made him happy. One day, he decided to travel farther than usual, away from his home, to discover something special. As he roamed, the wild smelly bear found a delicious berry bush and decided to stay and eat some of the yummy fruit. After eating all of the berries, the bear was so full that he decided to take a nap in a sunny clearing. When the bear woke up, it was dark outside and he realized it was time to return home. He ran and ran, until he eventually arrived back at his nice, cozy cave. The bear was so happy to be home, he smiled and curled up to take another nap. The end! In general, given two numbers formula_1 and formula_2, the exponentiation of formula_1 and formula_2 can be written as formula_5, and read as "formula_1 raised to the power of formula_2", or "formula_1 to the formula_2th power". Other methods of mathematical notation have been used in the past. When the upper index cannot be written, people can write powers using the ^ or ** signs, so that 2^4 or 2**4 means formula_10. Having concluded, that Candye should be their harbour for entertainment, they made sale of some few inheritances, which lay the readiest for their purpose, as also the goods in their Houses, and then, under colour of venting Merchandises abroade; they bought a nimble Pinnace, fortified with good strength and preparation, and waited but for a convenient wind. On the other side, _Ninetta_, who was sufficiently acquainted with the forwardnesse of her Sisters desires and her owne; had so substantially prevailed with them, that a good voyage now was the sole expectation. Whereupon, the same night when they should set away, they opened a strong barred Chest of their Fathers, whence they tooke great store of gold and costly Jewelse, wherewith escaping secretly out of the House; they came to the place where their Lovers attended for them, and going all aboard the Pinnace, the windes were so furtherous to them; that without touching any where, the night following they arrived at _Geneway_. There being out of peril or pursuite, they all knit the knot of holy wedlocke, and then freely enjoyed their long wished desires, from whence setting sayle againe, and being well furnished with all things wanting; passing on from Port to Port, at the end of eight dayes they landed in _Candie_, not meeting with any impeachment by the way. People who are pro-choice believe that women should be allowed to have control over their own bodies when it comes to ending or continuing a pregnancy. They believe that, because the embryo or fetus is inside the woman's body and does not have developed enough organs to survive on its own until later in the pregnancy, it is not yet a person with rights. Pro-choice people also make the argument that abortion needs to be legal in order to protect women, because when abortion is illegal, it does not completely stop abortions from happening, but makes it so that women try to do abortions on themselves or get them done by people who are not trained doctors, which puts those women in danger of death or injury. Pro-choice people believe the way to prevent abortion is to make sure women only get pregnant when they want to. In addition to advocating the legality of abortion, pro-choice groups like Planned Parenthood often try to improve people's access to things used to prevent pregnancy (called "contraception"), and try to teach young people about sex to reduce the number of teen pregnancies. There are many additives to make concrete set faster, set slower, set stronger, reduce corrosion and so on. The Romans discovered that adding volcanic ash produced a concrete which would set under water. The Romans also knew that adding horse hair made concrete less liable to crack as it set, and that adding blood made it more frost-resistant. Attracting customers for free with attraction marketing is an effective way to grow your business without breaking the bank. This type of marketing focuses on creating valuable content that draws potential customers to you, rather than relying solely on traditional outbound methods like cold-calling or buying leads. The following steps will guide you through implementing this strategy: ### Step 1: Define Your Target Audience Before you can attract customers, you need to know who they are. Identify their demographics (age, gender, location), interests, needs, pain points, and online behavior. Understanding your target audience allows you to create tailored content that resonates with them and addresses their specific challenges. Conduct market research using tools such as Google Analytics, social media insights, and customer surveys to gather information about your ideal clients. Key tip: Be as specific as possible when defining your target audience. A narrow focus enables you to craft highly relevant messages that speak directly to their unique concerns and desires. Guideline: Spend time getting to know your audience so you can build trust and rapport with them more effectively. ### Step 2: Establish Your Brand Identity Version 1.03, released in August 1986, included enhancements that made it consistent with the international release like drivers for European keyboards and additional screen and printer drivers. She resented the insult, and ran immediately to her father, and, with a great deal of aggravation, complained of her brother, particularly for having acted so effeminate a part as to look in a glass, and meddle with things which belong to women only. And we have some little ones that are little tiny ones that he likes to carry around. Introduction: This lesson will follow a lesson teaching perspective. The students know how objects appear to go back in space. They also learned how to make objects appear to have volume. Motivation: I will start the class introducing the term, pointillism. Pointillism is the use of tiny, dot-like brushstrokes of contrasting colors to represent the play of light in a painting. The students will understand pointillism. The students will learn basic facts about Georges Seurat and his work, "The "Maria" at Honfleur". The students will look at pictures of ships and use their understanding of perspective, from the previous lesson, to draw ships, docks, marinas, etc. The students will discuss the different parts of ships, stern, mast, ropes, deck, anchor, etc. The students will use markers to use the technique of pointillism in their work of art. The students will engage in a group critique of their works of art. 1. I will start the class by having half of the students come up and look at a small portion of the print up close. Then, of course, have the other half do the same. The students will discuss what they saw, small dots of color. 2. I will then uncover the rest of the print. The students will then see that the small dots of color to create an image. This is when I would explain pointillism. 3. I would then introduce the artist, Georges Seurat, and the title of the print. 4. I will talk about the artist in further detail. For example, how he was the founder of Neo-Impressionism, his influences, background information, etc. 5. If the class has no questions or anything to add to the discussion of Seurat, I will then discuss the print. This will lead into a conversation of ships. I will pass out pictures of ships as a reference. I will make a list on the board of the parts of a ship from the information I gather from the students. 6. I will then tell the children to gather around my desk. I will then explain to the students their assignment. I will start by showing examples of previous students work and an example I created. 7. The students will draw a ship, in perspective, with a sea-like environment around it. I will briefly remind them how objects appear to get smaller as they go back into space. They will be told to draw lightly so later the pencil lines will be erased. I will demonstrate at my desk. 8. I will then explain how to use pointillism to add color and volume to their ships. Remind them how objects cast shadows and have light cast on then from the direction of the sun. 9. The students will then return to their seats as I pass out the paper and markers. The students will then create their works of art as I walk around helping children individually. 10. At the very end of class each student will hang their images on a bulletin board and put the markers back in the boxes and neatly place them on the cart. The students will then sit on the floor in front of the bulletin board. Closure: Their images will stay hanging on the bulletin board. We will then hold a group critique. I would ask the students if they like the technique of pointillism. I am sure the students will have conflicting opinions, which is good. Evaluation/Assessment: The evaluation of the work will be a group discussion. We will discuss who did a good job using perspective and pointillism and what was difficult to do using this technique or what might have been easy and enjoyable. This will allow students to reflect on their work of art and voice their opinions of the lesson. This can also act as learning experience for myself to see how to improve the lesson for future use. Title: How to Clean Gunk from Your Dog's Eyes Dogs can accumulate gunk in their eyes due to various reasons such as eye irritation, allergies, or tear duct problems. This build-up is not only unsightly but also potentially harmful if left untreated. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to clean your dog's eyes safely and effectively. **Step 1: Gather Necessary Supplies** Before you start, make sure you have these items ready: 1. A container of saline solution (preferably sterile) or warm water. 2. Cotton balls or gauze pads. 3. A clean washcloth or towel. 4. Treats to reward your pet. Using a saline solution is recommended because it closely resembles your dog's natural tears and won't cause any harm even if it gets into their eyes. However, warm water can be used in a pinch. Avoid using soap or shampoo unless directed by a vet, as they can sting and lead to further issues. **Step 2: Calm Your Dog** Calming your dog down before starting is crucial. You want them to associate this process with positive experiences, so try to keep things relaxed and fun. Speak softly, offer treats, and lots of praise throughout the process. **Step 3: Prepare the Eye Area** Wash your hands thoroughly to prevent introducing bacteria into your dog's eyes. Then, gently lift your dog's eyelid with your thumb. Be careful not to touch the actual eyeball. **Step 4: Apply the Solution** Soak a cotton ball or gauze pad in the saline solution or warm water. Wring it out slightly so it's damp but not dripping wet. Gently wipe away the discharge starting at the inner corner of your dog's eye moving outward. Use a new section of the cloth for each swipe to avoid spreading the discharge back onto the cleaned area. Don't force the eyelids open wider than necessary; just enough to remove the debris. **Step 5: Reward Your Dog** After cleaning one eye, give your dog a treat and plenty of verbal praise. Repeat the process for the other eye, again offering rewards afterwards. Remember, patience is key here. If your dog becomes agitated, take a break and resume later when they are calmer. The goal is to make this a stress-free experience. **Step 6: Monitor Your Dog's Eyes** Regularly check your dog's eyes for signs of redness, excessive tearing, squinting, or continued build-up. If these symptoms persist, consult with your veterinarian. They may recommend specialized products or prescription medications depending on the underlying condition causing the issue. By following these steps regularly, you can help maintain your dog's eye health and ensure they're seeing the world clearly and comfortably. Regular checks and gentle cleansings can go a long way towards preventing serious eye problems down the line. Just like they do in the garage, right? So what did you do today Eric? Imagine you have a magnetic pinwheel sitting on your desk. The blades of the pinwheel are spinning around really fast, creating a whirlwind or swirling motion in the air around it. Now, let's say you want to measure the strength and direction of this wind movement along different closed paths near the pinwheel. In our example, the wind movement can be thought of as a 2D vector field, where each point in the field has both a speed (length of the vector) and a direction (direction of the arrow). To understand the behavior of this vector field, we can use something called Stoke's Theorem. This theorem helps us simplify calculations when dealing with certain types of vector fields. Let me try my best to put Stoke's Theorem into easier terms! Think of it like this - instead of directly measuring the wind movement along various wiggly closed curves, we will transform these path measurements into area measurements over surfaces bounded by those paths. By doing so, calculating becomes more manageable since areas tend to be less complex than curvy paths. Now, imagine drawing two shapes next to your spinning pinwheel; one being a square ($C_1$) consisting of four straight lines connecting points (0,0), (a,0), (a,b), and back to (0,0); while another being just a straight line segment ($C_2$) linking points (0,0) and (a,b). You notice that there seems to be the same amount of wind moving across each shape despite their differences. That's because Stoke's Theorem tells us that under specific conditions, the total wind movement (or line integral) around any closed path remains constant regardless of its complexity. The magic behind this lies within the fact that changes happening inside the surface (rotations in our case) contribute equally to the overall outcome even if the outer boundary varies. So whether you choose a complicated squiggle or a straightforward line, the end result stays the same due to the consistent wind patterns generated by the spinning pinwheel. And that, folks, is the essence of Stoke's Theorem applied to a simple 2D vector field! Isn't it fascinating how mathematical concepts can describe real-world phenomena so elegantly? Title: How to Change a User's Login Picture in Windows XP Windows XP allows you to customize your login picture, also known as the user account picture, which appears when you log into your computer or switch users. This tutorial will guide you through changing a user's login picture with clear, step-by-step instructions. **Step 1:** Access the Control Panel ---------------------------------- Click **Start**, then select **Control Panel**. The Control Panel is where various system settings are managed. It provides an interface that enables you to modify configurations according to your preferences. **Step 2:** Choose 'User Accounts' ------------------------------- In the Category View (default), click on **User Accounts**. If you can't find this option, change the view by clicking on **Switch to Classic View** at the top left corner of the window, then double-click the **User Accounts** icon. **Key Tip:** Changing views does not affect the functionality; choose whichever layout makes navigation easier for you. **Step 3:** Manage Another Account --------------------------------- Under the Pick a task section, click on **Create a new account** if you want to add a new user first before changing their picture. Otherwise, click on **Change my picture** next to your current username. **Guideline**: Only administrators can create or manage other accounts. There are several types of flea including the dog flea, cat flea, human flea, northern rat flea, and the oriental rat flea. During the Middle Ages, the oriental rat flea spread a bacteria that caused the Bubonic Plague, also known as the Black Death or Black Plague, a huge epidemic much more severe than today's bird flu. The oriental rat flea would become infected by the bacteria "Yersinia pestis". After infection the bacteria grew inside of the flea, eventually blocking the path to its stomach. Because of this, the flea was always hungry. When the flea would bite a human it opened up the skin but since the stomach of the flea was blocked, the blood from the human was heaved up back into the human, only this time it was infected with the Bubonic plague bacteria. Title: Exploring Special Numbers and Patterns with a Cool Twist on Counting! Hello young mathematicians! Today we are going to have some fun exploring a special way of working with our beloved counting numbers (that's just another name for the natural numbers - 1, 2, 3, 4, etc.). We will discover cool patterns using "open sets" and see how this helps us form unique groups of numbers called "dense" sets. Let's dive into this exciting mathematical adventure! First, let's imagine having baskets filled with apples numbered from 1 upwards. Now, I want you to pick any basket and remove all the apples in it except one. That single apple left behind represents an "open set," which means it has a special property: when you add up the reciprocal values (1 divided by) of all the remaining apples in other baskets, it will never go beyond a certain finite total value! In math language, we say the series converges (it reaches a limited final amount). Isn't that neat? Now comes the interesting part where we create groups of these special "open sets." These groups are known as "( , )-dense" sets. Imagine placing your apples along a line so each apple has room for its own little friends nearby. This group of neighboring apples forms a dense set since there are infinitely many opportunities to choose more apples while still keeping them close together. And here's the catch - no matter which apples you select from your dense set, their corresponding reciprocal series always DIVES (meaning it goes on forever without ever settling down!) because of those endless choices you made. So now you know how to identify open sets among your apples and gather them into fascinating dense collections! By doing this, you learned something incredible: whenever you come across a collection of natural numbers whose reciprocal series diverge, you can rest assured knowing that they miraculously fit the definition of being "( , )-dense!" How amazing is that?! Keep practicing forming these peculiar open sets and gathering them into dense families. Soon enough, you'll start noticing intriguing connections between different sets and uncover deeper secrets hidden within these deceptively simple tasks! Happy counting and remember to share your findings with me; I would love to hear about your extraordinary adventures! It had been sweet to mark the passage of that light bark freighted with these happy lovers, when borne by its sails it swept through the little ocean-channel that lies between the beaked promontories of the mainland and the closing cliffs of the island of Nisida; and when with gentler motion it glided into the open expanse of the Bay of Baia, and cut its way through the translucent water, above the ruins of temples and palaces overgrown by seaweed, on which the rays of the sun were playing, creating a thousand rainbow hues, that varied with every wave that flowed over them. In all that plane of blue light it was the only moving thing; and as if it had been the child of the ocean that bore it, and the sun that looked down on it, it sped gaily along in their smiles past the fortress where Brutus and Cassius sought shelter after the death of C  sar; past the temples of Jupiter and Neptune; by the ruins of that castle in which three Romans once portioned out the world between them, to the Cumean hill that enshadows the beloved Linternum of Scipio Africanus, and in which he died. The whole of this coast is a paradise of natural beauty, investing with its own loveliness the time-eaten wrecks with which it is strewn; the mouldering past is mingled with the vivid present; ruin and grey annihilation are decked in eternal spring. The woody windings of the shore reveal, in their deep recesses, the gleaming marble fragments of the abodes of ancient heroes; the verdurous hues of the promontories mingle with the upright columns of shattered temples, or clothe, with nature's voluptuous bloom, the pale funereal urns of departed gods; whilst the foliage and the inland fountains, and the breaking waves upon the shore, were murmuring around their woven minstrelsy of love and joy. Earth, sea, and sky blazed like three gods, with tranquil but animated loveliness; with a splendour that did not dazzle-with a richness that could not satiate. Creating an imaginary animal can be a fun and creative exercise that allows you to let your imagination run wild. This activity can help improve your problem-solving skills, stimulate your creativity, and provide a sense of accomplishment. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to create an imaginary animal: **Step 1: Research Different Animals** Before creating your own imaginary animal, research various animals from around the world. Familiarize yourself with their physical characteristics, habitats, diets, behaviors, and adaptations. This will give you a solid foundation to build upon when designing your unique creature. Key tip: Take notes or draw sketches as inspiration strikes. **Step 2: Determine Your Animal's Habitat** Decide where your imaginary animal lives. Is it terrestrial, aquatic, or arboreal? What kind of climate does it inhabit - tropical rainforest, desert, tundra, or something else entirely? The environment plays a crucial role in shaping an animal's appearance and behavior. Guideline: Consider what features would aid survival in its chosen habitat. **Step 3: Design Its Physical Characteristics** Now it's time to sketch out your creature's appearance. Think about combining traits from several real animals to form one extraordinary being. For instance, you might mix the body structure of a lion, the wingspan of an eagle, and the colorful scales of a reptile. Remember, there are no limits here! Tip: Make sure your design is balanced; too many limbs or oversized features could hinder movement or functionality. **Step 4: Define Its Dietary Needs** What does your imaginary animal eat? Carnivores consume meat, herbivores eat plants, while omnivores dine on both. You may also consider if your animal is a hunter, scavenger, or filter feeder. These choices impact other aspects of your creature's biology, such as teeth shape, jaw strength, and digestive system. **Step 5: Establish Its Social Structure** Is your animal solitary or social? Does it live alone or in groups called packs, flocks, schools, or herds? A species' social structure influences its communication methods, mating rituals, and defense strategies. Keep in mind that some animals change their social habits based on age, sex, or season. **Step 6: Develop Unique Abilities or Adaptations** Every animal has unique abilities or adaptations that help them survive in their specific environments. Perhaps your imaginary animal has camouflage capabilities, glows in the dark, communicates through vibrations, or uses echo location. Be imaginative but plausible. **Step 7: Name Your Creation** Choosing a name adds personality to your imaginary animal. It should reflect its appearance, behavior, or origin. Have fun with this process - brainstorm words associated with your creature's characteristics until you find the perfect fit. **Step 8: Write a Description** Write a brief description detailing your imaginary animal's looks, lifestyle, and special traits. Pretend you're writing an encyclopedia entry for this newfound species. Use scientific terminologies to lend authenticity. **Step 9: Share Your Creation** Share your imaginary animal with others. They might offer interesting insights or suggestions, sparking further development. Plus, showing off your creation can bring joy to those who see it. Remember, creating an imaginary animal is all about unleashing your creativity and exploring possibilities beyond our natural world. Enjoy the journey of bringing your unique creature to life! A computer program is a list of instructions that tell a computer what to do. Everything a computer does is done by using a computer program. Programs stored in the memory of a computer ("internal programming") let the computer do one thing after another, even with breaks in between. John von Neumann, a mathematician born in Hungary, came up with this idea in the late 1940s. The first digital computer designed with internal programming capacity was the EDVAC (which means Electronic Discrete Variable Automatic Computer), built in 1949. Where do you think he's goin'? Ben and Lily were twins who liked to play with their toys. One day, they found a big box in the garage. It was heavy and had a label that said "Do not open". Ben and Lily were curious and wanted to see what was inside. They pushed and pulled the box until they moved it to the living room. They used a scissors to cut the tape and opened the box. Inside, they saw a lot of shiny things that looked like stars. They were happy and started to take them out and play with them. But the shiny things were not stars. They were fireworks that their dad had bought for a special night. The scissors made a spark when they cut the tape, and the spark lit one of the fireworks. The firework made a loud noise and flew out of the box. It hit the curtains and set them on fire. Ben and Lily were scared and ran away. They heard more noises and saw more fire. They hid under the bed and cried. Their mom and dad came home and saw the smoke and the flames. They called the firemen and tried to put out the fire. The firemen came and stopped the fire. They asked Ben and Lily what happened. Ben and Lily felt guilty and told them the truth. They said they were sorry and that they did not know the shiny things were dangerous. Their mom and dad were angry and sad. They gave Ben and Lily a big hug and said they were glad they were safe. Achieving a bikini-ready stomach requires dedication, discipline, and consistency in both exercise and nutrition. This comprehensive guide will provide you with an in-depth understanding of the necessary steps to sculpt your abdominal muscles and maintain a healthy diet. **Step 1: Set Realistic Goals** Before embarking on any fitness journey, setting realistic goals is crucial. Determine what you hope to accomplish, whether that's losing fat, building muscle, or improving overall health. Remember, everyone's body responds differently to exercises and diets; therefore, patience and persistence are vital. **Step 2: Understand Abdominal Anatomy** The rectus abdominis (the 'six-pack'), obliques (side abs), and transverse abdominis (deep core) make up the abdominal region. Each muscle group requires specific exercises to strengthen and tone effectively. **Step 3: High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT)** Incorporate HIIT workouts into your routine two to three times per week. These sessions should include compound movements like burpees, mountain climbers, high knees, etc., which engage multiple muscle groups simultaneously, including the core. HIIT boosts metabolism and promotes fat loss, revealing defined abs over time. **Step 4: Strength Training** Add strength training exercises targeting the core muscles twice weekly. Planks, Russian twists, bicycle crunches, leg raises, and weighted sit-ups can help build strong, visible abs. Compound lifts such as squats, deadlifts, and bench press also engage the core stabilizers. **Step 5: Cardiovascular Exercise** Engage in regular cardio activities like running, swimming, cycling, or dancing five days a week for at least 30 minutes each session. Cardio aids in burning calories and reducing body fat percentage, making your abdominals more visible. **Step 6: Proper Nutrition** No amount of exercise can outdo a poor diet. To get a bikini-ready stomach, focus on consuming lean proteins, complex carbohydrates, fruits, vegetables, and healthy fats while limiting processed foods, added sugars, and saturated fats. Portion control is equally important. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** - **Stay Hydrated**: Drink plenty of water throughout the day to aid digestion, keep energy levels high, and prevent dehydration during workouts. - **Consistency is Key**: Consistent effort towards exercising and eating right yields results. Don't expect immediate changes overnight. - **Rest Days Matter**: Allow yourself rest days between intense workout sessions to avoid injury and promote recovery. - **Progressive Overload**: Gradually increase intensity, volume, or resistance in your workouts to continually challenge your body and stimulate growth. - **Balance Your Macros**: Balancing your daily intake of protein, carbs, and fats ensures optimal bodily function and supports muscle development. - **Mindful Eating**: Pay attention to what you eat, when you eat, and why you eat. Avoid emotional or mindless eating habits. - **Sleep Well**: Quality sleep plays a significant role in muscle recovery and hormonal balance, impacting physical performance and weight management. Remember, every person's body type and response to exercise and diet vary. Be patient with yourself and celebrate small victories along the way. With determination and adherence to this guide, you'll be well on your way to achieving a bikini-ready stomach! What kind of whale is that? " Good night cow jumping over the moon. [Chi holds base upside down with everything attached.] [experimenter may be talking to Megan or SS] AT the time of the Revolution there were but few people living on the north side of the Ohio River. But there were many Indians there. These Indians killed a great many white people inKentucky. The Indians were sent by British officers to do this killing. There was a British fort at Vincennes in what is now Indiana. There was another British fort or post at Kaskaskia in what is now the State of Illinois. George Rogers Clark was an American colonel. He wanted to stop the murder of the settlers by the Indians. He thought that he could do it by taking the British posts. He had three hundred men. They went down the Ohio River in boats. They landed near the mouth of the Ohio River. Then they marched a hundred and thirty miles to Kaskaskia. Kaskaskia was far away from the Americans. The people there did not think that the Americans would come so far to attack them. When Clark got there, they were all asleep. He marched in and took the town before they waked up. The people living in Kaskaskia were French. By treating them well, Clark made them all friendly to the Americans. When the British at Vincennes heard that Clark had taken Kaskaskia, they thought that they would take it back again. But it was winter. All the streams were full of water. They could not march till spring. Then they would gather the Indians to help them, and take Clark and his men. But Clark thought that he would not wait to be taken. He thought that he would just go and take the British. If he could manage to get to Vincennes in the winter, he would not be expected. Clark started with a hundred and seventy men. The country was nearly all covered with water. The men were in the wet almost all the time. Clark had hard work to keep his men cheerful. He did everything he could to amuse them. They had to wade through deep rivers. The water was icy cold. But Clark made a joke of it. He kept them laughing whenever he could. At one place the men refused to go through the freezing water. Clark could not persuade them to cross the river. He called to him a tall soldier. He was the very tallest man in Clark's little army. Clark said to him, "Take the little drummer boy on your shoulders." The little drummer was soon seated high on the shoulders of the tall man. " Now go ahead!" said Clark. The soldier marched into the water. The little drummer beat a march on his drum. Clark cried out, "Forward!" Then he plunged into the water after the tall soldier. All the men went in after him. They were soon safe on the other side. At another river the little drummer was floated over on the top of his drum. At last the men drew near to Vincennes. They could hear the morning and evening gun in the British fort. But the worst of the way was yet to pass. The Wabash River had risen over its banks. The water was five miles wide. The men marched from one high ground to another through the cold water. They caught an Indian with a canoe. In this they got across the main river. But there was more water to cross. The men were so hungry that some of them fell down in the water. They had to be carried out. Clark's men got frightened at last, and then they had no heart to go any farther. But Clark remembered what the Indians did when they went to war. He took a little gunpowder in his hand. He poured water on it. Then he rubbed it on his face. It made his face black. With his face blackened like an Indian's, he gave an Indian war-whoop. The men followed him again. The men were tired and hungry. But they soon reached dry ground. They were now in sight of the fort. Clark marched his little army round and round in such a way as to make it seem that he had many men with him. He wrote a fierce letter to the British commander. He behaved like a general with a large army. After some fighting, the British commander gave up. Clark's little army took the British fort. This brave action saved to our country the land that lies between the Ohio River and the Lakes. It stopped the sending of Indians to kill the settlers in the West. The system of rail transport was invented in England, so it has the oldest railway network in the world. It was built mostly during the Victorian era. The British Rail network is part privatised, with privately owned train operating companies providing service along particular lines or regions, whilst the tracks, signals and stations are owned by a Government controlled company called Network Rail. In some religions, the devil is a bad spirit or supernatural being that tries to create problems for people and distance them from God. Some people also use the words "the Devil" or "Satan" for the most powerful devil. The word "devil" comes from the Greek word "diabolos" which means "someone who tells lies to hurt you". Title: How to Delete Apps on Windows Windows operating system allows users to install various applications to enhance productivity, entertainment, or other specific purposes. However, as time goes by, you may find that some apps are no longer needed, taking up valuable storage space. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting apps on your Windows device. ### **Part 1: Deleting Apps from Start Menu** Step 1: Access the Start Menu Click on the Windows icon at the bottom left corner of your screen or press the Windows key on your keyboard. The Start Menu will appear displaying all installed applications. Key Tip: If you cannot see an application tile in the Start Menu, click on "All apps" link at the bottom of the menu to view all installed applications in alphabetical order. Why this works: The Start Menu serves as a central hub for launching and managing installed programs. By accessing it, you can quickly locate and remove unwanted apps. -------------------------------------------------------- Step 2: Locate the App You Want to Remove Scroll down until you find the app you wish to delete. Remember, if you have many applications, using the search bar located at the top center of the Start Menu could speed up the process. Key Guideline: Be sure you've identified the correct application before proceeding with removal. Check its name, publisher, and icon thoroughly. Why this matters: Accidentally removing the wrong application might lead to unintended consequences such as data loss or disruption of functionalities. -------------------------------------------------------- Step 3: Right-click on the Application Tile Once you've found the right application, right-click (or hold touchpad button) on the app tile. A contextual menu should pop up providing several options including "Uninstall". I don't know how to draw a mouth. Mom can you cut it in half I can't eat it big. Title: How to Find Percentage Change Introduction: In this article, we will provide you with an in-depth guide on finding percentage change between two numbers or values. Understanding percentage change is essential in various fields such as finance, statistics, physics, engineering, and many other areas where quantitative analysis plays a crucial role. This skill allows you to compare changes in data over time, helping you make informed decisions based on accurate evaluations. By following our clear, concise steps, you'll master determining percentage change quickly and effectively. Step 1: Identify the Initial Value (Before) To calculate percentage change, first identify your initial value-the original number or amount before any alteration takes place. This serves as the basis for comparison when assessing fluctuations in numerical data. For example, if you want to find out the percentage increase in sales from January to February, then January's sales figure would be your initial value. Key Tip: Always ensure that both values are expressed using the same unit of measurement, making comparisons more precise and meaningful. Step 2: Determine the Final Value (After) Next, establish your final value-this represents the new quantity after some form of modification has occurred. Following the previous example, February's sales figures would represent the final value since they reflect the state of affairs following the period under consideration. Guideline: Just like Step 1, verify that both values share identical units so that calculations remain accurate and relevant. Step 3: Calculate the Difference Between Values Now, subtract the initial value from the final value to determine their difference. The result may either indicate an increase (positive value) or decrease (negative value), depending on whether the final value surpasses or falls short of its predecessor, respectively. Continuing with our earlier illustration, compute the disparity between February and January's sales revenues. Key Tips: Make sure to perform arithmetic operations correctly; otherwise, subsequent percentages could become misleading or erroneous. If calculating by hand, jot down intermediate results to avoid potential mistakes during mental computations. Step 4: Obtain Absolute Difference Since percentage changes should always adhere to positive values, take absolute value (remove negative sign if present) of the computed discrepancy obtained in Step 3. This ensures consistency while computing percentage variations irrespective of underlying trends (increasing vs decreasing). Using our running analogy, transform the calculated sales distinction into an absolute value. Step 5: Divide Absolute Difference by Initial Value Divide the resulting absolute difference derived in Step 4 by the initial value established in Step 1. This division operation provides insight into how much the final value deviates proportionally from the starting point. Keep practicing proper mathematical techniques here, ensuring correctness throughout every stage of calculation. Step 6: Multiply Quotient by 100 Lastly, multiply the quotient acquired in Step 5 by 100 to convert it into a percentage format, which simplifies interpretation considerably. Aim for precision and accuracy during these multiplication exercises, double-checking outcomes whenever necessary. Formula Recap: Percentage Change = ((Final Value - Initial Value)/Initial Value) x 100% Conclusion: By meticulously executing all these sequential procedures, you can now confidently ascertain percentage differences between varying sets of numeric information. With consistent practice, this ability becomes second nature, enabling quicker judgments concerning fluctuating statistical patterns across numerous disciplines. Happy analyzing! Calculating the Hypotenuse of a Triangle: A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide The hypotenuse is the longest side of a right triangle, located opposite the right angle. Unlike other sides, you cannot directly measure the length of the hypotenuse when you have only two other sides' measurements. However, by using the Pythagorean theorem, you can calculate the exact length of the hypotenuse effortlessly. This article will guide you through calculating the hypotenuse of a triangle with clear explanations, helpful tips, and practical advice at every step. **What You Need:** 1. The lengths of the two legs (adjacent and opposite) of the right triangle. We will denote these as *a* and *b*. 2. Basic mathematical operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and square root calculation. No need for advanced knowledge or tools! **Instructions:** _Step 1: Identify the Two Legs of the Right Triangle_ Determine which sides are the adjacent and opposite sides relative to the right angle. Label them as *a* and *b*, where *a* represents the shorter leg and *b* denotes the longer one. If your triangle has different labels, simply adapt this method accordingly. Remember that we aim to find the hypotenuse, represented as 'c'. ![Right Triangle](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/e/eb/Calculate-the-Hypotenuse-of-a-Triangle-Step-1.jpg/aid457368-v4- Title: How to Cook Geoduck Geoducks (pronounced "gooey-ducks") are large, saltwater clams that are popular in Pacific Northwest cuisine. They have a unique taste and texture, making them an exciting ingredient to cook with. This tutorial will guide you through the process of preparing and cooking geoduck, providing detailed steps and helpful tips along the way. **Things You'll Need:** * Fresh geoduck * Large knife or cleaver * Cutting board * Bowl of ice water * Paper towels * Saucepan or wok * Vegetables, sauces, or seasoning as desired **Step 1: Purchase Fresh Geoduck** Search for reputable seafood markets or suppliers that specialize in live geoducks. Look for ones that appear lively and fresh - their siphons should be moving vigorously. Keep in mind that live geoducks can last up to a week refrigerated if kept moist and cold. **Step 2: Clean the Geoduck** Rinse off any dirt from the geoduck under cold running water. Place it in a bowl of ice water to keep it cool during cleaning. Remove its mantle by pulling gently at the edge where the shell meets the body. Discard the dark intestines attached to the mantle. Rinse thoroughly again to ensure all debris is removed. ![Cleaned Geoduck](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/7/7b/Clean-a-Geoduck-Clam-Step-3.jpg/aid459680-v4- But today he actually picked something up off the top. Title: How to Download a Video Off YouTube Downloading videos from YouTube can be useful for various reasons such as watching them offline, saving a backup copy, or creating compilations. However, it's important to note that downloading copyrighted material without permission is illegal and unethical. Always ensure you have the necessary rights before proceeding with this guide. This tutorial will walk you through the process using a desktop computer; mobile devices require different steps which aren't covered here. We'll use a free online service called "savefrom.net" due to its ease-of-use and wide compatibility. 1. **Find the video you want to download on YouTube.** This step seems obvious, but remember that only videos whose creators allow downloading should be downloaded. You can check if this is the case by looking under the video player where it usually says 'Share', 'Save', or similar. If these options are available, then there's a good chance the creator has enabled downloads. 2. **Copy the video URL.** Once you've found your video, click on the address bar at the top of your browser (where the website address appears). The entire web address (URL) of the page should now be highlighted. Right-click (or Ctrl+A on Windows / Command+A on Mac) to select the whole URL, then right-click again and choose 'Copy'. 3. **Go to savefrom.net.** Open a new tab or window in your browser and navigate to <https://en.savefrom.net/>. SaveFrom Net is an ad-supported site, so expect some popups or banner ads - just close them and continue. 4. **Paste the video URL into the input box.** On the SaveFrom Net homepage, you'll see a large input box asking for the URL of the video you wish to download. Right-click inside this box and select 'Paste', or simply press Ctrl+V (Command+V on Mac) to paste the previously copied URL. 5. **Choose the format and quality.** After pasting the URL, wait a moment while SaveFrom analyzes the link. When ready, it presents several options including different formats (MP4, WEBM, etc.) and qualities (ranging from low resolution like 144p up to high definition like 1080p or even 4K). Select the one most suitable for your needs. Keep in mind higher resolutions mean larger file sizes. 6. **Click 'Download'.** Having chosen your preferred format and quality, click the green 'Download' button next to it. Your browser may ask whether you really want to leave the current page since you're about to navigate away from it - confirm that you do. 7. **Confirm the download.** Back in the main window, another dialog box might appear warning you that the file could harm your computer because it comes from an external source. Since we know what we're doing, dismiss this message by clicking 'Keep'. 8. **Wait for the download to complete.** Depending on the length and quality of the video, along with your internet speed, the download time will vary. Be patient and let it finish completely before attempting to open the file. And there you have it! By following these eight steps, you've successfully downloaded a video off YouTube. Remember to always respect copyright laws and video creators' wishes when deciding which videos to download. [as Bro turns to a page with a tree] He wants to go outside and play with ball. Title: How to Change Your AirDrop Ringtone AirDrop is a feature introduced by Apple that allows you to wirelessly transfer files between iOS devices and Macs. When someone tries to AirDrop something to your device, it emits a distinctive sound to alert you. If you find this default ringtone annoying or want to differentiate it from other alerts, you can change the AirDrop ringtone. This tutorial will guide you through changing your AirDrop ringtone using an iPhone as an example. The steps are similar for iPads and Macs with slight modifications based on their respective user interfaces. **Step 1:** Access Settings App --------------- Locate the gray gear icon labeled "Settings" on your home screen or app library and tap it to open. The settings app contains various customization options for your device's functionality. **Step 2:** Navigate to Sounds & Haptics --------------------------------------- Scroll down until you see "Sounds & Haptics," then select it. Here, you manage all system sounds like keyboard clicks, lock sounds, and more importantly, our focus - the AirDrop tone. **Step 3:** Scroll Down to "AirDrop" Section ------------------------------------------ Within the 'Sounds & Haptics' menu, continue scrolling until you reach the "AirDrop" section. It should be near the bottom but above 'Ringtones.' **Step 4:** Tap on "AirDrop" Sound Button ---------------------------------------- Tapping on the word "AirDrop" under the "Alert Tones" category opens up a list of available tones. These include both built-in ones provided by Apple and any additional tones downloaded from the internet or synced via iTunes. **Key Tip**: To sync custom tones purchased outside of the Apple ecosystem, connect your iPhone to your computer running iTunes, navigate to the 'Tones' tab, check 'Sync Tones,' and choose the folder containing your downloaded tones. After synchronizing, these new tones will appear in the list when editing your AirDrop sound. **Step 5:** Choose New AirDrop Ringtone ------------------------------------ Browse through the list and listen to previews by pressing play next to each option (indicated by a small rightward arrow). Once you've decided on a new AirDrop tone, simply tap on it to set it as your AirDrop sound. **Guideline**: Make sure to pick a tone distinct enough from others so that you can quickly identify when someone is trying to AirDrop something to your device. **Step 6:** Confirm Selection --------------------------- After selecting a new AirDrop ringtone, press the back button at the top left corner of the screen twice to return to the main 'Sound & Haptic' settings page. You should now notice that the previously selected AirDrop tone appears under the 'ALERT TONES > Airdrop' category. **Step 7:** Exit Settings App --------------------------- To finalize changes, hit the blue 'Done' button located at the upper right corner of the screen. Then, close the settings app either by swiping up from the bottom edge if you have an iPhone X or later, or by clicking the Home button if you own an older model. Congratulations! You have successfully changed your AirDrop ringtone following our comprehensive guide. Now every time someone initiates an AirDrop request, you'll hear your chosen tune instead of the default one. Enjoy personalizing your device further while making file sharing even more convenient! Let's see what animals are in there. Okay maybe we can look for a scooter sometime. It's cords and cords are so exciting. And she's got dolls because she had a granddaughter. When babies are firstborn they don't eat big kid food like you, they just drink milk. [enters BRO's room and brings him out] = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PC-ac/bla33.cha = = = Title: Figure Out If Someone Likes You Over Text: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Deciphering whether someone likes you through text messages can be challenging due to the impersonal nature of this form of communication. However, with careful observation and analysis, you can identify subtle signs that indicate their feelings towards you. This guide will help you understand how to figure out if someone likes you over text by breaking down various steps and providing insightful tips along the way. Step 1: Pay Attention to Frequency and Timing The frequency and timing of their responses can provide valuable insights into their level of interest in you. * Key Tip: If they respond quickly and frequently, it may suggest that they enjoy conversing with you and are interested in getting to know you better. On the other hand, slow or infrequent replies could mean they're uninterested or distracted. Step 2: Observe Initiation Patterns Take note of who starts conversations most often. * Guideline: When someone consistently initiates conversations, there is a strong likelihood they like you because they want to connect with you. Conversely, if starting conversations feels one-sided, they might not have as much romantic interest. Step 3: Analyze Content and Depth of Conversation Delve deeper into what your text exchanges entail. * Explanation: Topics revolving around personal experiences, aspirations, and emotions tend to signify an emotional connection between individuals. Sharing intimate details implies trust and comfort - indicators of affectionate feelings. Step 4: Look Out for Flirtatious Language Flirting via text usually involves playfulness, teasing, compliments, and sexual innuendos (when appropriate). * Important Note: Be aware that flirting styles vary from person to person; some people may express themselves subtly while others are more direct. Understanding individual differences here is crucial. Step 5: Identify Emotional Investment Emotionally charged language indicates a higher degree of investment in the relationship. * Insight: Expressions of excitement, enthusiasm, curiosity, concern, or sympathy reflect genuine care and emotional attachment. These elements can manifest through exclamation marks, emojis, questions, well wishes, etc. Step 6: Recognize Persistence Persistent attempts at making plans despite cancellations or rejections signal continued interest. * Caution: While persistence can be endearing, ensure it doesn't cross into pressuring behavior, which is generally considered disrespectful and counterproductive. Step 7: Evaluate Consistency Across Different Platforms Compare their interactions with you across different platforms such as social media, email, or messaging apps. * Rationale: Consistent positive engagement suggests sincere affections, whereas inconsistencies might imply fluctuating levels of interest or attention. Conclusion: While determining if someone likes you over text requires patience and keen observation, remember that texts don't always paint the whole picture. People communicate differently based on context, mood, personality traits, cultural background, and technological familiarity. Therefore, combining these observations with face-to-face interaction cues will give you a more accurate understanding of someone's feelings towards you. Choosing an email name may seem like a simple task, but it is actually quite important as it serves as your online identity when communicating through emails. A well-chosen email name can make you appear more professional, memorable, and trustworthy. This tutorial will guide you through the process of choosing an effective email name, with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Determine Your Purpose Before selecting an email name, consider its intended purpose. Are you creating this account for personal or professional reasons? If it's for business purposes, you might want to include keywords related to your industry or company name. For personal accounts, feel free to be creative while maintaining some level of formality and appropriateness. Key tip: Avoid using overly complicated words or phrases that could confuse recipients about who they are really interacting with. Step 2: Check Availability Once you have decided on your preferred format (e.g., firstname.lastname@domain.com), check if it is available across various platforms such as Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo Mail, etc. You can do this by visiting their respective websites and entering your chosen handle into the signup form. It is advisable to secure the same username across multiple platforms to maintain consistency and avoid confusion. Guideline: Try adding numbers or underscores if your initial choice isn't available, but keep these additions minimal and logical. Step 3: Consider Using Your Full Name Using your full name (firstname.lastname) is often considered the most professional approach, especially for work-related correspondence. However, depending on your name's uniqueness and length, there might be potential issues with availability. Key tip: Keep in mind cultural differences regarding names; what works in one country may not necessarily translate well internationally. Always opt for simplicity and clarity. Step 4: Use Keywords Related To Your Industry Or Profession If your primary goal is to establish a strong professional presence, incorporating relevant keywords into your email address can help boost credibility and searchability. For example, if you're a graphic designer named John Smith, you could try johnsmithgraphicdesigner@email.com. Step 5: Avoid Unprofessional Elements Steer clear from including any unprofessional elements in your email name, such as nicknames, slang terms, or offensive language. These choices can negatively impact others' perceptions of your communication skills and overall image. Guidelines: - Refrain from using all caps or lowercase letters excessively. - Limit the usage of special characters except for periods and underscores. - Stay away from numerical sequences unless absolutely necessary. Step 6: Test The Chosen Email Name After finalizing your email name, send test messages to friends, colleagues, or family members to get feedback on readability, memorability, and overall impression. Their input can provide valuable insights into whether your selected handle effectively represents you. Step 7: Secure Your Desired Email Name Across Social Media Platforms Consistency is crucial in establishing a solid online presence. Once you have settled on your ideal email name, ensure that it is also available as a username on popular social media sites like LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, etc. By doing so, people can easily find and recognize you across different channels. Following these steps will enable you to choose an appropriate, professional, and memorable email name that accurately reflects your personality or brand. Remember, taking the time to carefully select your email address goes a long way in building trust and making positive impressions upon those you communicate with digitally. Title: Find Out If a Person You're Interested In Is Already Taken Introduction: Determining whether a person you are interested in is already taken can be challenging but essential before expressing your feelings or making advances. This tutorial provides a comprehensive guide on how to find out if someone is single or committed, emphasizing respect, discretion, and ethical methods. Remember that everyone deserves privacy and honesty; thus, avoid deception or intrusive actions while following these steps. Step 1: Analyze Their Social Media Presence Examine their social media profiles (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, etc.) carefully for signs of a relationship. Look for couple photos, shared posts about romantic activities, or references to a significant other. Be cautious about jumping to conclusions based solely on this information as some people may choose not to display their relationships online. Key Tip: Pay attention to consistency across platforms. Discrepancies might suggest they prefer keeping their relationship status private. Step 2: Observe Their Daily Life If possible, observe their daily routine and interactions with others. Attend public events where they usually hang out, like local cafes, bars, or clubs. Notice who accompanies them frequently and how they interact with those individuals. While observing, ensure you maintain proper distance and do not invade their privacy. Guideline: Avoid stalking behavior. Maintain an appropriate level of observation without crossing boundaries or making the individual uncomfortable. Step 3: Engage in Casual Conversation Initiate casual conversations when opportunities arise. Ask open-ended questions related to hobbies, interests, or work to build rapport gradually. Subtly steer the conversation towards relationships by discussing movies, books, or friends' experiences involving romance. Gauge their reactions and responses to determine if they are currently involved with someone else. Key Tip: Listen attentively during conversations. People often reveal more than intended through body language and verbal cues. Step 4: Consult Mutual Friends or Acquaintances Reach out to mutual friends or acquaintances tactfully, asking if they know anything about the individual's relationship status. Make sure to approach trusted individuals who will respect confidentiality and refrain from gossiping. Only rely on secondhand information as a last resort since accuracy cannot be guaranteed. Guideline: Never ask mutually known friends to spy or pry into the person's life. Instead, request general knowledge about their dating history or current situation. Step 5: Reflect on Your Own Feelings and Actions Consider why discovering their relationship status is important to you. Are you seeking companionship, validation, or merely curiosity? Understanding your motivations allows you to act appropriately and considerately. Additionally, reflect upon your own values and expectations regarding relationships and commitment before pursuing further action. Key Tip: Honesty and transparency are crucial components of any successful relationship. Approaching potential partners with genuine intentions fosters trust and respect. Conclusion: Discovering a person's relationship status requires patience, subtlety, and respect for privacy. By analyzing social media presence, observing daily life, engaging in casual conversation, consulting mutual connections, and reflecting on personal feelings, you can make informed decisions about pursuing a connection. Always prioritize honesty, empathy, and integrity throughout this process. Aisha was a young girl. She is described as very intelligent with a very good memory. Find the number of which 79.5% is 101.Often, solving these kinds of problems is taught with the idea that you "translate" certain words in the problem into certain symbols, and thus build an equation. Solving that way, the unknown number would be Y, "of" would be multiplication, and "is" corresponds to '='. We'd get: Y 79.5% = 101. 0.795 Y = 101 Y = 101/0.795 = 127.044025157 I'm a bit leery of this method, as it's so mechanical. What if a question comes that is not worded exactly as the ones in the book, and the student just gets stuck? Or it is worded so that the student gets misled and calculates it wrong? So while this idea is great and works, it is also necessary for students to understand the concept of percent well. In the above problem, we are to find a number so that 79.5% of that number is 101. (Obviously, then, the number itself is more than 101.) If you understand the problem, and say the problem that way, it is pretty obvious how to write the equation: "79.5% of that number is 101"... so 0.795 Y = 101. Ideas for using MENTAL math for calculating percent problems I have also made a video of this topic. It shows you how to use mental math for calculating simple percentages. - Find 10% of some example numbers (by dividing by 10). - Find 1% of some example numbers (by dividing by 100). - Find 20%, 30%, 40% etc. of these numbers. FIRST find 10% of the number, then multiply by 2, 3, 4, etc. For example, find 20% of 18. Find 40% of $44. Find 80% of 120. I know you can teach the student to go 0.2 18, 0.4 0.44, and 0.8 120 - however when using mental math, the above method seems to me to be more natural. - Find 3%, 4%, 6% etc. of these numbers. FIRST find 1% of the number, then multiply. - Find 15% of some numbers. First find 10%, halve that to find 5%, and add the two results. - Calculate some simple discounts. If an item is discounted 20%, 15%, etc., then find the new price. - "40% of a number is 56. What is the number?" - types of problems. You can do this mentally, too: First FIND 10% and then multiply that result by 10, to find 100% of the number (which is the number itself). If 40% is 56, then 10% is 14. So 100% of the number is 140. This result is reasonable, because 40% of this number was 56, so the actual number (140) needs to be more than double that. - "34% of a number is 129. What is the number?" (Now you need a calculator.) You don't need to write an equation. You could also first find 1% of this number, and then find 100% of the number. If 34% of a number is 129, then 1% of that number is 129/34. Find that, and multiply the result by 100. I recently got this sort of homework question sent to me: I have a problem and I don't know how to solve it so here is the problem: At a popular clothing store clothes are on sale when they have hung on the rack too long. When an item is first put on sale, the store marks the prices down 30% off. If some shoes are regular-priced at $50.00, how much will they cost after the discount? You simply first find 10% of $50, then use that to find 30% of $50, and lastly subtract. Easy as a pie! (10% of $50 is $5. 30% of $50 is three times as much, or $15. Lastly subtract $50 - $15 = $35. So the discounted price is #$35.) And he came right up like this. Once upon a time, there was a bright guard who lived in a castle. Title: How to Construct a Simple Paper Airplane Introduction: Making a paper airplane is an enjoyable activity that can be done by people of all ages. It's simple, fun, and requires minimal materials - just one sheet of standard A4 or Letter-sized paper. This tutorial will guide you through constructing a basic dart-style paper airplane, which is easy to fly and suitable for both beginners and experienced papermakers. Materials Needed: - One piece of A4 (210mm x 297mm) or Letter-sized (8.5in x 11in) plain white printer paper Instructions: Step 1: Prepare Your Workspace Gather your material and clear a flat surface to work on. Make sure there are no distractions around so you can focus on creating your masterpiece. Lay down the sheet of paper with its shorter side facing you. Step 2: Fold Along the Centerline Fold the paper in half lengthwise, aligning the edges as closely as possible. Unfold the paper after making the crease; this fold serves as a reference line for subsequent folds. The folded edge should now run along the middle of the page, dividing it into two equal halves. Key Tip: Accurate folds ensure better flight performance. Take time to make precise creases. Step 3: Form the Body Take the top corner from either side of the paper and bring them together along the centerline crease made earlier. You should end up with a triangle shape. Press firmly along the new crease lines. Guideline: If the alignment isn't perfect, unfold and try again. Precision matters! Step 4: Create the Plane's Nose Perform another inside reverse fold starting at about 1cm below where the last fold ended. Tuck in the excess paper behind the previous folds. Repeat this process once more, moving slightly lower down the body of the plane. These steps create the pointed nose of the aircraft. Step 5: Establish Wing Angles With the remaining width of the paper, perform outside folds towards the centerline. Each wing should have roughly the same angle relative to the horizontal axis of the plane. Adjust these angles during testing later if necessary. Key Tip: Symmetrical wings improve stability, while asymmetry affects maneuverability. Step 6: Finalize the Wings Unfold each wing section away from the main body but keep the uppermost point connected. Now pull the wings downwards until they reach their natural position parallel to the ground. Crease the sides firmly to secure the wings in place. Step 7: Test Flight Preparation Ensure the wings are symmetrical and aligned properly. Hold the airplane between your thumb and index finger near the back end, keeping your grip light yet firm. Check that nothing is obstructing the path ahead before launching. Step 8: Launching Technique Bend your arm slightly at the elbow, then swing it forward swiftly yet smoothly. Release the airplane when your forearm becomes perpendicular to your upper arm. Practice makes perfect; adjust your throwing speed and technique based on observation. Conclusion: Congratulations! You've successfully constructed your own paper airplane. Remember, designing and building paper planes involve lots of trial and error. Feel free to tweak our design or explore other models online. Happy flying! That's right said Raggedy Ann with a big smile. If you want cake raise your hand. The House of Commons hold their meetings in the Palace of Westminster. Nina is used to going to sleep with a bottle.] [4.5 sec during which S lets out a little sob] In humans, sex is normally either male or female. For example: John's sex is male. Jane's sex is female. Most of the time, the sex of a person depends on what sex chromosomes that person got from his or her parents. A woman's ovum (egg cell) contains one X chromosome. A man's sperm contains either an X or a Y chromosome. When a sperm and ova combine to form a fertilised egg, the baby may get either of these chromosomes from its father. If the baby gets two X chromosomes, it will develop into a female. If the baby gets one X and one Y, it will develop into a male. Before babies are born, their sex parts are already formed, but not ready yet. During Puberty, the sex parts finish developing as well as other parts of the body like breasts and body hair. [uses yellow wand to pick up objects from the table; adds two more silver men to the circle on the black base] Ready, Set, Read! fluent readers, children need to learn how to read faster, smoother, expressively. Reading fluency is directly related to reading comprehension. Time and practice are essential for becoming a fluent reader. An excellent way to fluency is through rereading text. In addition, rereading text students to read more words per minute. -copy of 100 School Days by Anne Rockwell for each student -time sheet for each student: Your Name:_________________ Your Partner s Name:_________________ 1. You all are learning to become fluent readers, and the best way to do that is to practice reading. You are going to read the same text several times to improve your fluency. We will time each of your readings to see how well reading speed improves. As you reread the text, you will read it more expressively each time! Remember, you might not always know every word in the text, fluent readers do not always know every word either, but you should always read to the end of the sentence or use a silent cover-up method when you are stuck on a word. 2. Write this sentence on the board: On the first day of school Mrs. Madoff gave me a penny. Read the sentence slowly to the students to model a non-fluent reader. Reread the sentence expressively, smoothly, and quickly to model a fluent reader. Ask the students "Did you like the first or second reading better? Good! The second time I read with fluency by reading expressively, smoothly, and quickly. 3. Give the students a book talk about 100 School Days: This book is about a class and everyday a different student brought a penny to school. They would put the penny in a jar on the teacher s desk, but they did not know why she was telling them to do so. Let s read this book to find out what they did with all of their pennies. Read 100 School Days to the entire class and tell them to follow along so they can become familiar with all of the words. 4. Split the students into pairs. Each pair should have one copy of 100 School Days and each student should have a copy of the time sheet. Instruct students to write their name on their time sheet as well as the name of your partner. After you are finished, switch time sheets with your partner so that you have each other s time sheets. 5. You and your partner will take turns being the reader and the recorder. Begin by deciding who will be the reader first. Good! The reader is to start reading at the beginning of 100 School Days and read for 1 minute. I will stand at the front of the room with the stopwatch. When 1 minute is up, I will say STOP ! The reader should point to the word he/she stopped on. Then, the recorder will count the amount of words the reader read during that 1 minute. The recorder will then record that amount of words on the time sheet next to first time space. Are there any questions? Good. Are you ready? GO ! 6. The readers will read from the same spot again, start at the beginning. This time, the recorder will fill in the second time space. Are you ready? GO ! 7. STOP ! Did the number in the second time space get bigger than the number in the first time space? Good! The readers will read from the same spot again, start at the beginning. This time, the recorder will fill in the third time space. Are you ready? GO ! 8. STOP ! Did the number in the third time space get bigger than the number in the second time space? Terrific! Now it is time for you and your partner to switch roles. We will do the exact thing over again with the new readers and recorders. Are you ready? GO !" (Follow the same steps to enable the new reader to read the same passage three times while being timed and recorded.) 9. Talk to your partner about how well you did on your time sheet. Ask your partner if you read expressively, smoothly, and quickly. Would anyone like to share his or her good work? Great! I am glad to hear that you read faster, more expressively, and more accurately each time! You sound like a fluent reader! Remember that comprehension is our main goal and the more you read, the more fluent you will become! 10. The students will be assessed by individually coming to the teacher s desk for a one-minute read of 100 School Days. Each student's time will be recorded and compared to the student's time sheet. The new time and time sheet will be kept for future comparisons. The students can finish reading100 School Days silently while waiting to be assessed. 100 School Days by Anne Rockwell Buzz, Buzz, Buzz! By Katie Lincoln. Title: How to Enjoy Don't Starve Don't Starve is an engaging survival game that challenges players to survive in a wilderness environment filled with strange creatures and mysterious powers. This tutorial will guide you through enjoying Don't Starve by providing in-depth explanations, key tips, and guidelines for each step. By following these steps, you can increase your chances of surviving and have fun while doing so! ### Step 1: Understand the Game Mechanics Before starting the game, understand its basic mechanics. The primary goal is to survive as long as possible against dwindling health, hunger, sanity, and seasonal changes. Each resource category (health, hunger, and sanity) requires constant management throughout the game. * Health represents physical well-being; when depleted, characters die. Healing items or resting near fires replenish health. * Hunger measures food intake; if left unattended, it leads to starvation and eventually death. Consuming food satisfies hunger. * Sanity reflects mental stability; low sanity results in hallucinations and decreased effectiveness. Keeping busy, eating certain foods, and avoiding dark areas maintain sanity levels. (Tip: Familiarize yourself with all three meters and their respective colors - red for health, green for hunger, and blue for sanity.) ### Step 2: Choose Your Character Selecting the right character suits different playstyles. Some are better suited for beginners due to unique abilities and advantages. Here's an overview of available characters: 1. Wilson (default): Starts with a science machine and has moderate stats across all categories. 2. Willow: Has higher initial sanity but lower health and takes fire damage less frequently. Also starts with a lighter. 3. Wendy: Accompanied by her ghost friend Abigail who attacks enemies. Lower overall stats compared to others. 4. Wolfgang: Strongest character with high health and attack power but suffers severe penalties at low health and sanity. 5. Woodie: Transforms into a beast form after chopping down trees which increases strength and speed. Vulnerable to insanity from touching rotten logs. 6. Maxwell: Begins with a teleportation staff capable of transporting him short distances instantly. Low base stats except for sanity. 7. Wes: A frail gentleman with excellent research capabilities and a pet bird named Charlie. 8. WX-78: A robot prone to overheating during summer days yet highly resistant to freezing temperatures. Can upgrade using gears found around the world. 9. Wickerbottom: Knowledgeable about many things including crafting books that provide new recipes. However, she loses sanity quickly when reading them. 10. Then Sara yelled out to bobby it's okay. That has a snowman on the front, huh? If you're done why don't you leave now. From Campaign 2009: Lesson 3: MAPPING THE MOON Students use their map skills and follow clues to find fire-safety tips hidden throughout the classroom! To build skills in map reading, following directions, and fire safety. Mapping the Moon Worksheet 1. Before class, place direction signs on the four walls of the classroom: North, South, East, and West. Make four sets of safety tips on four different colors of paper. Place the tips around the room. Establish a starting point for each color and write simple direction clues to find them. For example: Walk seven steps east and ten steps south. What is message number one? Tips: - Keep space heaters three feet away from anything that can burn. - Grown-ups should stay in the kitchen when food is cooking on the stovetop. - Ask grown-ups to blow out candles before leaving a room. - Do not put too many plugs into an outlet. 2. Divide the class into four teams, one per color, and provide them with the clues. The first team to find all four safety tips wins. 3. Distribute copies of the Mapping the Moon Worksheet, and have students complete it. I'm the American hero who doesn't lose his instructions.
